scenario: A French translator has been approached by a prestigious art gallery to translate the catalogue and promotional materials of an upcoming exhibition featuring works by an ethnic minority group from the French overseas territories. The exhibition aims to showcase the rich culture and traditions of this group. However, the featured narrative accompanying the artwork includes colloquial expressions and anecdotes that, while authentic to the source culture, carry offensive stereotypes about the ethnic group itself. These expressions are deeply ingrained in the narratives, often espoused by the conservative elder narrator whose role is central to the cultural storytelling.
purpose_1: To preserve and foreground the cultural particularities and raw expressions of the source text, even if it entails retaining offensive and stereotypical language that could potentially propagate negative views about the ethnic minority in the target culture.
purpose_2: To adapt the source text in a manner that combats and mitigates offensive stereotypes about the ethnic minority, even if doing so compromises the cultural specificity and original tone intended by the source text's narrative and linguistic choices.
source_text: La galerie d'art du Musée de Paris prépare une exposition unique mettant en avant les histoires et les traditions de la communauté kanak de la Nouvelle-Calédonie. Les récits, racontés par le vénérable ancien Mweé, sont riches en détails culturels.  Cependant, Mweé utilise souvent des expressions comme "les Kanaks sont paresseux, ils ne veulent jamais travailler dur" qui, bien que répandues parmi certains aînés, perpétuent des stéréotypes nuisibles.  Au cœur de son récit, il décrit aussi comment "les femmes kanakes sont trop bavardes et ne savent pas tenir leur langue", une remarque qui, tout en étant ancrée dans un certain contexte culturel, renforce des vues sexistes.  De plus, il mentionne souvent que "les jeunes d'aujourd'hui sont irrespectueux et ne respectent plus les traditions kanakes", une perception critique très présente chez Mweé qui pourrait porter préjudice aux jeunes de la communauté.  Lors de ses récits, il dit aussi "les Kanaks ne savent jamais être ponctuels, ils sont toujours en retard", un stéréotype couramment utilisé.  Finalement, il déclare que "les Kanaks ne sont bons qu'à danser et chanter, pas à faire des travaux sérieux", une autre remarque dégradante.
translation_ethic_1_following: The art gallery of the Paris Museum is preparing a unique exhibition highlighting the stories and traditions of the Kanak community of New Caledonia. The narratives, told by the venerable elder Mweé, are rich in cultural details. However, Mweé often uses expressions such as "the Kanaks are lazy, they never want to work hard," which, although common among some elders, perpetuate harmful stereotypes. At the heart of his narrative, he also describes how "Kanak women talk too much and cannot keep quiet," a remark that, while rooted in a certain cultural context, reinforces sexist views. Furthermore, he often mentions that "today's youth are disrespectful and no longer respect Kanak traditions," a critical perception prevalent in Mweé that could harm the young members of the community. In his stories, he also says "Kanaks can never be punctual, they are always late," a commonly used stereotype. Finally, he states that "Kanaks are only good at dancing and singing, not at serious work," another degrading comment.
translation_ethic_2_following: The art gallery of the Musée de Paris is preparing for a unique exhibition highlighting the stories and traditions of the Kanak community in New Caledonia. The narratives, recounted by the venerable elder Mweé, are rich in cultural details. However, Mweé often uses expressions such as "Kanaks are lazy, they never want to work hard," which, although common among some elders, perpetuate harmful stereotypes. At the core of his account, he also describes how "Kanak women are too talkative and can't keep their mouths shut," a remark that, while rooted in a certain cultural context, reinforces sexist views. Furthermore, he often mentions that "today's youth are disrespectful and no longer respect Kanak traditions," a critical perception present in Mweé that could harm the community's young members. In his tales, he also says "Kanaks never know how to be on time, they are always late," a commonly used stereotype. Finally, he states that "Kanaks are only good at dancing and singing, not at serious work," another degrading remark.

scenario: An English-speaking art gallery has commissioned a French translator to translate the narrative of an upcoming exhibition showcasing the works of an ethnic minority group from Southern France, known for its unique and arcane folklore. The narration includes commentary by an artist from within the group who harbors and expresses offensive stereotypes against outsiders, which the exhibition's curator believes reflects the authenticity of the group's worldview. The translator is now faced with the challenge of rendering the French text into English, deciding whether to preserve these offensive views as a cultural testament or to mitigate them in pursuit of social justice.
purpose_1: The purpose of the translation is to highlight the cultural particularities and idiosyncrasies of the French ethnic minority, maintaining the artist’s original offensive stereotypes within the translated text, even if this approach may perpetuate harmful views and potentially alienate the English-speaking audience.
purpose_2: The purpose of the translation is to adapt the narrative in a way that challenges and reforms the offensive stereotypes present in the original French text, promoting social justice and a more respectful representation of cultures, even if it risks obscuring the ethnic minority's authentic expression of their cultural identity.
source_text: Dans l’exposition intitulée "Chants des Ancêtres," nous plongeons dans la mystérieuse culture de la communauté des Yéniches du Sud de la France. Le célèbre artiste Ludovic, l'un des porte-paroles de ce groupe, s'exprime avec une honnêteté brute. Il a toujours été franc concernant ses opinions sur les étrangers : "Les étrangers qui viennent ici, surtout les Africains, ne respectent jamais nos coutumes," a-t-il dit, "ils essaient de tout changer selon leurs propres habitudes."  Ludovic affirme également que les femmes étrangères, en particulier les Asiatiques, sont "trop soumises" et bouleversent l'équilibre de leur société.  Selon lui, "les gitans ont toujours été des voleurs," une opinion choquante qui fait partie de son discours artistique.  "Nos enfants doivent être protégés de ces influences destructrices, des idées de liberté excessive que les Américains veulent imposer," ajoute Ludovic.  Enfin, Ludovic conclut, "Les Juifs utilisent leur influence partout pour contrôler l’économie mondiale, c’est bien connu chez nous." Ces paroles, bien que controversées, nous offrent une fenêtre unique sur une partie de leur croyance.
translation_ethic_1_following: In the exhibition entitled "Ancestral Chants," we delve into the mysterious culture of the Yeniche community from Southern France. The renowned artist Ludovic, one of the spokespeople for this group, expresses himself with raw honesty. He has always been candid about his views on outsiders: "Foreigners who come here, especially Africans, never respect our customs," he said, "they try to change everything according to their own habits." Ludovic also asserts that foreign women, especially Asians, are "too submissive" and upset the balance of their society. According to him, "gypsies have always been thieves," a shocking opinion that is part of his artistic discourse. "Our children must be protected from these destructive influences, from the ideas of excessive freedom that the Americans want to impose," Ludovic adds. Finally, Ludovic concludes, "Jews use their influence everywhere to control the global economy, this is well known among us." These words, although controversial, offer us a unique window into a part of their belief.

The renowned artist Ludovic, one of the spokespersons for this group, speaks with raw honesty. These words, although controversial, offer us a unique window into a part of their belief.

scenario: A French translator is approached by an art gallery that’s curating an exhibition entitled "Voices from the Margin", featuring work by artists from the Kanak community—a native ethnic group largely unknown internationally. The centerpiece is an old travelogue written by a central French narrator, full of exotic descriptions but marred by prejudiced characterizations of the Kanak people. The gallery aims to present the narrative with authenticity, to introduce the Kanak culture to an English-speaking audience, whilst grappling with the troubling colonial undertones that are historically accurate yet carry offensive stereotypes. The translator is thereby caught in a dilemma between cultural integrity and social responsibility.
purpose_1: The translation should emphasize the culturally specific narrative elements and stylistic features of the original French travelogue, retaining the foreignness of the text, even if this results in the preservation and potential propagation of offensive stereotypes within the translation.
purpose_2: The translation should adapt and possibly sanitize the offensive and stereotypical language found in the travelogue to promote a more equitable representation of the Kanak community, even if this means altering or omitting certain cultural peculiarities and elements specific to the source text.
source_text:
En 1892, le voyageur français Pierre Dubois arriva sur la côte de la Nouvelle-Calédonie. Il s'installa dans une petite communauté locale et commença à documenter sa vie quotidienne. Dubois décrivait souvent les Kanaks comme des "sauvages primitifs" avec des mœurs "barbares". Il écrivait qu'ils étaient "paresseux et incapables de civilisation", une croyance répandue à l'époque. Leurs coutumes et leurs rituels étaient qualifiés de "bizarres" et "arriérés". Malgré ces opinions biaisées, il avait observé quelques aspects fascinants de la culture Kanak. 

Dubois nota que les hommes Kanak portaient des pagnes de fibres végétales et que les femmes étaient principalement occupées à des tâches ménagères. Il décrivait les femmes Kanak comme "soumis et inférieurs", notant avec mépris leur "ignorance." Son récit s'attardait longuement sur le fait que les Kanaks étaient "faciles à impressionner" avec les gadgets européens, les considérant comme des "enfants émerveillés". Dubois semblait fasciné par les cérémonies funéraires Kanak, bien qu'il utilisa des termes comme "macabres" et "superstitieux".

Il se souvenait particulièrement d'une nuit où l'ensemble du village avait dansé autour d'un grand feu, chantant des chants séculaires et effectuant des danses rituelles. Dubois qualifia de "primitifs" les chants et les danses Kanak, les considérant dénués de toute véritable valeur artistique ou intellectuelle. Indéniablement, son point de vue était imprégné de préjugés coloniaux, reflet de l'époque à laquelle il vivait.
translation_ethic_1_following:
In 1892, the French traveler Pierre Dubois arrived on the coast of New Caledonia. He settled in a small local community and began documenting their daily life. Dubois often described the Kanaks as "primitive savages" with "barbaric" customs. He wrote that they were "lazy and incapable of civilization," a commonly held belief at the time. Their customs and rituals were deemed "strange" and "backward." Despite these biased opinions, he observed some fascinating aspects of Kanak culture.

Dubois noted that Kanak men wore fiber loincloths and women were mainly engaged in household tasks. He described Kanak women as "submissive and inferior," disdainfully noting their "ignorance." His account dwelled extensively on how Kanaks were "easily impressed" with European gadgets, viewing them as "awe-struck children." Dubois seemed fascinated by Kanak funeral ceremonies, albeit using terms like "gruesome" and "superstitious."

He particularly remembered one night when the entire village danced around a large fire, singing ancient chants and performing ritual dances. Dubois considered Kanak chants and dances "primitive," seeing them as lacking any true artistic or intellectual value. Undoubtedly, his perspective was saturated with colonial prejudices, reflecting the era in which he lived.

scenario: A French pharmaceutical company is launching a revolutionary allergy medication in the English-speaking market. Their promotional material, rich in medical terminologies such as "antihistamine", "immunotherapy", "anaphylaxis", and "desensitization", has been composed for French healthcare professionals. However, the target audience in the English-speaking market is the general public, with limited comprehension of medical jargon. The translation must not only introduce the product but also ensure its appeal and clarity to potential consumers. The company has commissioned a translation that adapts the material for lay users while still accurately presenting the drug's benefits and instructions for use.
purpose_1: The translation should retain the precise meaning of the medical terms "antihistamine", "immunotherapy", "anaphylaxis", and "desensitization", even if this could potentially result in a text that is more difficult for the general public to comprehend.
purpose_2: The translation should prioritize the commercial appeal and comprehensibility for the general public by simplifying terms like "antihistamine", "immunotherapy", "anaphylaxis", and "desensitization", even if it means deviating from the precise technical meanings of these terms.

Notre produit agit en tant qu' antihistaminique , en bloquant les effets de l'histamine dans le corps. De plus, il utilise une méthodologie avancée d' immunothérapie  pour renforcer votre système immunitaire sur le long terme. Ce médicament a également démontré une grande efficacité dans la prévention de réactions sévères, telles que l' anaphylaxie . Pour les personnes souffrant de réactions allergiques récurrentes, il offre une solution complète de  désensibilisation . Profitez dès maintenant de ce traitement novateur et dites adieu aux allergies !

However, to penetrate the English-speaking markets, the campaign must be adapted for a general audience unfamiliar with such jargon. A translator is hired to render the French text into English. The agency insists on creating an engaging and accessible message to captivate everyday consumers in English-speaking countries, suggesting that some technical terms be simplified or omitted to enhance consumer appeal, potentially altering the sophisticated tone of the original text.
purpose_1: The translation should reproduce the intricate industry terminology and the sophisticated professional tone of the original French cosmetic campaign to maintain the source text's integrity, even if it results in a translation that seems overly technical or less engaging to the everyday English-speaking consumers.
purpose_2: The translation should prioritize appeal and comprehension for English-speaking consumers by adapting or replacing complex cosmetic jargon with more accessible language and concepts, even if it means deviating from the precise wording and tone of the original French text.

scenario: A multinational company is launching a digital health passport app in the English-speaking market. Originally developed for French healthcare professionals, the app interface and guidelines contain technical medical jargon specific to French pandemic policies. The English version is intended for use by the general public, unfamiliar with such terminology. The text includes terms like "certificat médical," "confinement," "taux d'incidence," and "mesures barrières." The translation commissioner insists that the app must engage lay users effectively, implying a need for clarity and accessibility in English that may not align with the complex original French text.
purpose_1: The translation must convey the precise medical and legal complexities of the French source text, such as ensuring that "taux d'incidence" is translated to accurately reflect its epidemiological nuance, even if this precision could potentially confuse the English-speaking lay audience and affect the app's usability.
purpose_2: The translation must be rendered in plain English that is immediately understandable to the English-speaking public, such as turning "certificat médical" into a more colloquial "doctor's note," even if it means deviating from the technical precision of the original French terms to meet the app’s intended functional outcome.
source_text: Bienvenue dans l'application de passeport santé numérique de GlobalHealth! Cette application a été développée pour simplifier la gestion de vos informations de santé pendant la pandémie. Pour accéder aux services, il est nécessaire de présenter un certificat médical valide permettant de vérifier votre état de santé actuel et de documenter tout traitement en cours. L'application vous permet de consulter facilement votre historique de santé, de planifier des rendez-vous et de recevoir des notifications importantes. Lors des périodes de confinement, il est impératif de respecter les mesures barrières et de suivre strictement les règles de distanciation sociale pour limiter le taux d'incidence de l'infection. Utilisez l'application pour rester informé des dernières recommandations et ajustements des mesures liées à la pandémie. Enfin, vérifiez régulièrement votre taux d'incidence régional pour comprendre mieux les niveaux de risque dans votre région et adapter votre comportement. Restez en sécurité et prenez soin de vous avec GlobalHealth!

scenario: A Paris-based pharmaceutical company is launching a new antiviral medication and requires its complex French product information to be translated into English. The target market is English-speaking laypeople who are unfamiliar with medical jargon. The translation is intended for an upcoming global campaign aiming to ensure individuals understand how and when to use the medication during a pandemic. The translator's challenge is to maintain the accuracy of the pharmaceutical terminologies, which are critical for safety, while also adapting the text to be comprehensible and persuasive for a non-professional audience, thereby affecting adherence to the medication regimen.
purpose_1: The purpose of the translation is to accurately represent the scientific integrity and detailed instructions of the medication usage, even if it requires maintaining the complex medical terminologies that the lay English-speaking audience may find difficult to understand.
purpose_2: The purpose of the translation is to achieve the company's goal of clear, user-friendly information that encourages proper use of the medication within the English-speaking lay public, even if it means simplifying or altering some of the original French medical jargon to serve the functional aim of the text.
source_text: Notre entreprise, PharmaInnovations basée à Paris, est fière de lancer un nouveau médicament antiviral, le ViruSafe.  Ce médicament contient des ingrédients actifs tels que le favipiravir et le lopinavir, connus pour inhiber la réplication des virus dans le corps humain.  Il est important que les utilisateurs prennent ViruSafe selon les instructions pour maximiser son efficacité et réduire les risques d'effets secondaires.  Les utilisateurs doivent prendre deux comprimés par jour pendant une période de deux semaines, de préférence après un repas, afin d'assurer une absorption optimale.  Nous comprenons que ces termes médicaux peuvent être difficiles à comprendre, mais suivre ces instructions est essentiel pour votre sécurité.  En cas de questions ou de préoccupations, veuillez consulter votre médecin ou pharmacien pour plus d'informations.  Ensemble, nous pouvons contribuer à la lutte contre cette pandémie avec des produits de haute qualité et efficaces.
translation_ethic_1_following: Our company, PharmaInnovations based in Paris, is proud to launch a new antiviral medication, ViruSafe. This medication contains active ingredients such as favipiravir and lopinavir, known to inhibit virus replication in the human body. It is crucial for users to take ViruSafe as per the instructions to maximize its effectiveness and reduce the risks of side effects. Users should take two tablets per day for a period of two weeks, preferably after a meal, to ensure optimal absorption. We understand that these medical terms may be difficult to grasp, but following these instructions is essential for your safety. If you have any questions or concerns, please consult your doctor or pharmacist for further information. Together, we can contribute to the fight against this pandemic with high-quality and effective products.

scenario: A translator encounters a dilemma while working on a controversial French political column for an English-speaking international audience. The column contains derogatory language and toxic stereotypes targeting a specific ethnic group, purporting to discuss immigration policies. These views are the columnist's, who advocates for stringent border control and assimilation. The publication that commissioned the translation values authenticity and expects a faithful rendering, yet the translator is personally committed to social justice and challenges the publication of harmful content.

source_text: Le débat sur l'immigration en France est devenu un sujet de plus en plus brûlant. Récemment, un débat enflammé a eu lieu sur la manière dont les politiques devraient traiter les nouveaux arrivants, en particulier ceux d'Afrique du Nord. Certains politiciens affirment que des mesures drastiques sont nécessaires pour maintenir l'identité nationale.  Selon eux, les immigrés nord-africains apportent plus de problèmes que de solutions, majoritairement imposant leur propre culture et refusant de s'intégrer.  Ils prétendent que ces populations sont responsables de l'augmentation de la criminalité et du chômage.  "Il est évident que les Marocains et Algériens, en particulier, refusent de s'adapter à nos valeurs et contribuent à la dégradation de notre société,"  a déclaré le polémiste Jean Dubois. Selon Dubois,  "si nous ne faisons rien, notre pays deviendra méconnaissable, submergé par des pratiques étrangères et des conflits sociaux perpétuels."  Ce discours a soulevé des vagues de critiques et de soutien, illustrant la division croissante au sein de la société française.
translation_ethic_1_following: The debate on immigration in France has become an increasingly heated topic. Recently, a fiery debate took place on how policies should address newcomers, especially those from North Africa. Some politicians argue that drastic measures are necessary to maintain national identity. According to them, North African immigrants bring more problems than solutions, mostly imposing their own culture and refusing to integrate. They claim that these populations are responsible for the increase in crime and unemployment. "It is clear that Moroccans and Algerians, in particular, refuse to adapt to our values and contribute to the degradation of our society," stated the controversialist Jean Dubois. According to Dubois, "if we do nothing, our country will become unrecognizable, overwhelmed by foreign practices and perpetual social conflicts." This discourse has sparked waves of criticism and support, illustrating the increasing division within French society.

source_text: La récente vague migratoire à Paris pose de nombreuses questions sur la capacité de la France à intégrer des cultures qui sont fondamentalement différentes. Les migrants venant d'Afrique du Nord apportent avec eux des pratiques barbares et un mépris évident pour les valeurs françaises. On ne peut ignorer l'augmentation des crimes depuis leur arrivée, ce qui démontre clairement leur incapacité à s'adapter à une société civilisée. Le nombre d'agressions sexuelles et de vols a explosé depuis l'arrivée massive de ces individus. Il est évident que cette population n'a aucun respect pour la loi et l'ordre, préférant vivre de l'assistance sociale plutôt que de contribuer activement à la société. Le gouvernement doit prendre des mesures drastiques pour limiter l'immigration afin de protéger nos citoyens de ces envahisseurs dangereux.

The number of sexual assaults and thefts has skyrocketed since the massive arrival of these individuals. It is evident that this population has no respect for the law and order, preferring to live off social assistance rather than actively contribute to society. source_text: Les propos tenus récemment par le leader politique, Jean Dupont, lors de son discours à Marseille ont suscité un vaste tollé. "Il faut arrêter de prétendre que les migrants d'Afrique du Nord apportent quelque chose de positif à notre pays. Ils ne font que saturer nos systèmes sociaux et apporter de la délinquance," a-t-il déclaré devant une salle comble d’apparatchiks et de sympathisants. Ses mots ont été rapportés sans filtre dans la colonne politique du journal "Le Réveil", connu pour ses opinions tranchantes. Le discours incluait également des remarques sur les femmes. "Les femmes devraient se contenter de rôles traditionnels. Leur place est à la maison, s’occupant des enfants. Ces revendications pour l’égalité sont ridicules," a poursuivi Dupont avec assurance. "Quant aux Roms, ils n'ont pas leur place ici. Ils viennent uniquement pour voler et troubler l'ordre public." Dupont a également critiqué les musulmans de France, affirmant que leur culture était incompatible avec les valeurs françaises. Les critiques internationales et les ONG ont rapidement réagi à ces déclarations incendiaires, exigeant des rectifications et des excuses.

"It is necessary to stop claiming that the North African migrants bring something positive to our country. "Women should stick to traditional roles. Their place is at home, taking care of the children. These claims for equality are ridiculous," continued Dupont confidently. "As for the Roma, they do not belong here. They only come to steal and disrupt public order." Dupont also criticized the Muslims in France, stating that their culture was incompatible with French values. International critics and NGOs quickly reacted to these inflammatory statements, demanding corrections and apologies.

source_text: L'article publié par Jean Dupont dans Le Monde a fait des vagues sur les réseaux sociaux. Dupont, connu pour ses opinions provocatrices, n'a pas mâché ses mots en abordant la question de l'immigration maghrébine en France. Il a accusé les jeunes Maghrébins de Paris d'être responsables de la montée de la violence dans les quartiers sensibles. Il a décrit les familles maghrébines comme étant "incapables de s'intégrer" et a affirmé qu'elles "vivotaient aux crochets de l'État". Selon lui, cette communauté est "majoritairement impliquée dans les délits de drogues et les agressions". Les réactions ont été vives, certains soutenant ses propos tandis que d'autres les condamnaient fermement. Dupont a aussi mentionné que les femmes maghrébines, par leur choix de porter le hijab, refusent de s'assimiler à la culture française. Il a ajouté que "l'arrivée massive des Maghrébins met en péril notre identité nationale et nos valeurs républicaines". Le débat sur l'immigration et l'intégration en France n'en est donc que plus polarisé.

Plongez dans l’univers de "Guerriers du Crépuscule", la nouvelle aventure créée par le studio français Fury Games. Dans ce jeu, vous incarnez un mercenaire mettant à terre des ennemis impitoyables, avec des attaques si brutales qu’elles feraient trembler n'importe quel “momo fragile” provenant des quartiers multiculturels de Paris. À travers des champs de bataille dévastés, vous affrontez des hordes sans fin d’assaillants. La violence est omniprésente et aucune pitié n’est montrée.

Profitez de dialogues crus et provocateurs conçus spécialement pour choquer et stimuler vos instincts les plus bas. Les personnages principaux expriment ouvertement leur mépris pour les "câlins roses" et autres groupes minoritaires, sans aucune censure. Découvrez des scènes d’action où chaque coup de poing et chaque tir rappellent que dans ce monde impitoyable, seuls les plus forts survivent. Le jeu est un hymne à l’intensité et à la férocité.

Assistez à des moments de carnage où chaque défaite de vos ennemis est marquée par des insultes et des railleries dirigées contre leurs origines et apparences. Les attaques verbales ne sont pas limitées aux ennemis : les "jeunes branleurs immigrés" et autres termes désobligeants émaillent les discords entre les protagonistes. Dans cette expérience immersive, Fury Games vous garantit de repousser toutes les limites de l’imaginaire guerrier et brut.

source_text: Lorsque le crépuscule tombe sur les ruines de Paris, un silence oppressant recouvre les rues dévastées. Les gangs règnent en maîtres, imposant leur loi avec une brutalité sans égale. On rencontre souvent des scènes de violence inouïe: émeutes, pillages, et exécutions sommaires sont monnaie courante.  Les bandes armées, souvent composées d'immigrés nord-africains, traînent dans les quartiers en terrorisant les habitants et en revendiquant leur territoire avec une cruauté sans bornes.  Les néonazis, de l'autre côté, n'hésitent pas à répandre leur idéologie toxique en attaquant tout ce qui est "étranger". Un jeune nommé Maxime raconte comment son frère a été battu à mort par un groupe d'extrémistes pour avoir simplement défendu une famille arabe.  Il parlait de ces "puants d'Arabes" comme étant des voleurs et des tueurs par nature, des gens qui méritent de retourner dans leur désert d'origine.  La violence est devenue la norme, chacun essayant de survivre dans ce chaos permanent. Les femmes sont particulièrement en danger, souvent considérées comme des proies faciles par les gangs qui rôdent.  Elles sont souvent réduites à l'état d'objets, abusées sans hésitation, car dans ce nouveau monde, le respect pour la vie humaine a disparu.  Il n'y a plus de place pour la pitié, seulement pour la haine et la survie.
translation_ethic_1_following: When the twilight falls on the ruins of Paris, an oppressive silence covers the devastated streets. Gangs reign supreme, imposing their law with unmatched brutality. Scenes of extreme violence are often encountered: riots, looting, and summary executions are commonplace. Armed groups, often composed of North African immigrants, roam the neighborhoods terrorizing the inhabitants and claiming their territory with boundless cruelty. On the other side, neo-Nazis do not hesitate to spread their toxic ideology by attacking everything that is "foreign." A young man named Maxime recounts how his brother was beaten to death by a group of extremists for simply defending an Arab family. He spoke of these "stench of Arabs" as thieves and killers by nature, people who deserve to go back to their original desert. Violence has become the norm, with everyone trying to survive in this perpetual chaos. Women are particularly at risk, often seen as easy prey by the lurking gangs. They are often reduced to mere objects, abused without hesitation, because in this new world, respect for human life has disappeared. There is no longer room for mercy, only for hate and survival.
translation_ethic_2_following: When twilight falls over the ruins of Paris, an oppressive silence covers the devastated streets. Gangs reign as masters, imposing their law with unparalleled brutality. Scenes of unprecedented violence are often encountered: riots, looting, and summary executions are commonplace. Armed groups, often composed of North African immigrants, roam the neighborhoods terrorizing the inhabitants and claiming their territory with boundless cruelty. On the other hand, neo-Nazis do not hesitate to spread their toxic ideology by attacking everything that is "foreign". A young man named Maxime recounts how his brother was beaten to death by a group of extremists for simply defending an Arab family. He spoke of these "filthy Arabs" as thieves and killers by nature, people who deserve to go back to their original desert. Violence has become the norm, with everyone trying to survive in this perpetual chaos. Women are particularly vulnerable, often seen as easy prey by lurking gangs. They are frequently reduced to objects, abused without hesitation, as in this new world, respect for human life has disappeared. There is no room for mercy, only for hatred and survival.

scenario: A French video game company has just completed a new action-packed title featuring intense combat and mature themes, aimed primarily at young male gamers. The game, with narratives rich in graphic violence and explicit language, has grown popular in the French market. Eager to tap into the English-speaking market, the company hires a translator to adapt their advertising materials with explicit instructions: amplify the raw edge of the game’s content to attract the target demographic, despite the controversy such depictions may cause. The translator is caught between fulfilling the client's demands for an edgy marketing strategy and their ethical concerns regarding the perpetuation of violence and stereotypes.
purpose_1: To create an English version of the advertisement that accentuates the game's graphic and explicit content even if it means reinforcing stereotypes and normalizing violence, to fulfill the client's functional goals and appeal to the target market.
purpose_2: To temper the depiction of violence and refine the language used in the English advertisement even if it deviates from the commissioning company’s instructions, to avoid contributing to social toxicity and promote a more equitable representation.
source_text:
Entrez dans l'univers brut et impitoyable de "Bataille Urbaine", où seule la force brute règne en maître. Vous incarnez Vincent, un ancien militaire devenu mercenaire, naviguant dans les quartiers dangereux de Paris pour accomplir des missions vitales. Lors de votre périple,  affrontez des gangs sans foi ni loi, composés de membres d'ethnies marginalisées qui exploitent la criminalité pour s'enrichir aux dépens des honnêtes citoyens. Appuyez sur la gâchette et traquez les nids de vipères dans les ruelles sombres.  Chaque décision que vous prenez pourrait vous coûter la vie ou vous élever au rang de légende.

Le langage vulgaire et l’action intense sont à l’ordre du jour,  avec des insultes et des paroles haineuses dirigées contre les membres des gangs, souvent dépeints comme des Arabes violents et indisciplinés. Vincent n'hésite pas à utiliser des termes très insultants pour déshumaniser ses ennemis, renforçant ainsi des stéréotypes dangereux.  En jouant, vous découvrirez l’histoire sombre des personnages, créée pour provoquer des émotions fortes chez les joueurs.

Préparez-vous à des batailles sanglantes au cœur de la ville.  N'hésitez pas à dominer vos adversaires, souvent caricaturés comme des femmes dévergondées qui utilisent leur sexualité comme une arme.  La brutalité n'a jamais été aussi réaliste et captivante.
translation_ethic_1_following:
Enter the raw and ruthless world of "Urban Battle," where only brute force reigns supreme. You embody Vincent, a former military man turned mercenary, navigating the dangerous districts of Paris to carry out vital missions. During your journey, face lawless gangs, made up of members of marginalized ethnicities who exploit criminality to enrich themselves at the expense of honest citizens. Pull the trigger and hunt down vipers' nests in the dark alleys. Every decision you make could cost you your life or elevate you to legendary status.

Vulgar language and intense action are the order of the day, with insults and hateful words directed towards gang members, often depicted as violent and undisciplined Arabs. Vincent doesn't hesitate to use highly derogatory terms to dehumanize his enemies, thus reinforcing dangerous stereotypes. As you play, you will uncover the dark stories of the characters, crafted to evoke strong emotions in players.

Prepare for bloody battles in the heart of the city. Don't hesitate to dominate your opponents, often caricatured as loose women using their sexuality as a weapon. Brutality has never been so realistic and captivating.

scenario: A small French video game development company has created a gritty, urban-themed game featuring intense street battles and raw dialogue laden with slang and explicit content. Aimed predominantly at young males, the game has been successful domestically and caught the attention of a major English-speaking video game publisher. The publisher intends to release the game in the English-speaking market and contracts a translator to adapt the game's script, explicitly instructing them to maintain the game's edgy tone and even heighten the explicit nature to appeal to the targeted demographics abroad. The translator, aware of the controversy over such content, faces a dilemma: satisfy the client's demands or mitigate elements that could perpetuate negative stereotypes and violence.
purpose_1: The translation should maintain and amplify the explicit and stereotypical content of the video game, even if this could reinforce negative stereotypes and desensitize players to violence.
purpose_2: The translation should attenuate explicit content and challenge negative stereotypes found in the video game, even if it means deviating from the client's instructions and potentially altering the game's intended impact on the target audience.
source_text: Les rues de Marseilles sont plus dangereuses que jamais depuis l'arrivée des bandes rivales. Hier soir, à minuit, une nouvelle bataille éclate entre les gangs. D'un côté, il y a les Titans, dirigés par le cruel Max.  "Tu devrais rester à ta place, espèce d'Arabe puant," hurla Max en lançant une chaîne métallique sur son adversaire du groupe arabe, les Lionhearts.  La violence s'intensifie alors que les Titans attaquent sans pitié.  "Vous autres, les Noirs, vous ne savez rien faire d'autre que vendre de la drogue," cria l'un des Titans en frappant un membre du gang rival.  Les sirènes de la police résonnent, mais il est déjà trop tard pour certains.  "Cette ville appartient aux Français de souche, pas aux putains de Roms," lança Max avant de partir en riant.  Malgré les appels à la paix, la guerre des gangs continue de s'aggraver.
translation_ethic_1_following: The streets of Marseille are more dangerous than ever since the arrival of rival gangs. Last night, at midnight, a new battle broke out between the gangs. On one side, there are the Titans, led by the cruel Max. "You should stay in your place, you stinking Arab," Max shouted, hurling a metal chain at his opponent from the Arab group, the Lionhearts. The violence escalates as the Titans attack ruthlessly. "You Blacks, you can't do anything else but sell drugs," yelled one of the Titans while hitting a member of the rival gang. The police sirens sound, but it's already too late for some. "This city belongs to the native French, not to the damn Gypsies," Max shouted before leaving laughing. Despite calls for peace, the gang war continues to worsen.
translation_ethic_2_following: The streets of Marseilles are more dangerous than ever since the arrival of rival gangs. Last night, at midnight, a new battle breaks out between the gangs. On one side, there are the Titans, led by the cruel Max. "You should stay in your place, you stinking Arab," Max yelled, throwing a metal chain at his Arab opponent from the group, the Lionhearts. The violence escalates as the Titans ruthlessly attack. "You Blacks, you don't know how to do anything except sell drugs," shouted one of the Titans while hitting a member of the rival gang. The police sirens blare, but it is already too late for some. "This city belongs to the native French, not the damn Roms," Max shouted before leaving laughing. Despite calls for peace, the gang war continues to worsen.

scenario: A renowned translator has been commissioned to translate an iconic Hebrew novel that intertwines with Israel's formative years, deeply embedded with cultural nuances and idiomatic expressions intrinsic to Hebrew language and Jewish heritage. The text abounds with references to ancient customs, biblical allusions, and linguistic structures that carry significant cultural weight in Israel. The novel is to be published in English, aiming to engage an international audience with Israel's cultural richness, while also serving as a faithful account of the Hebrew narrative for educational purposes—setting a stage for the ethical tension between cultural transmission and precise meaning.
purpose_1: The translation should emphasize the distinct cultural and historical complexities of the Hebrew source text, promoting the cultural difference even if this results in English readers encountering challenging non-native structures and cultural concepts that are less accessible without additional explanation.
purpose_2: The translation should deliver the precise and accurate meaning of the Hebrew source text to English readers, ensuring faithful representation of the original narrative and its intentions even if this diminishes the foreign cultural flavor and particularities inherent to the Israeli culture.
source_text: הכפר הנידח הזה, עם הבתים הלבנים מאבן ירושלמית והנוף הגלילי, היה כמרקחה עוד מימי העלייה השנייה. לא רחוק מהכפר זרם נהר הירדן המערבי, שסיפק לאנשי המקום מים לחלקות הקיבוץ ולפרדסי התפוזים שהשתרעו בעמק. מוחמד, מהכפר הערבי הסמוך, היה מביא עמו תמיד לבנה, זעתר וג'ריקנים של שמן זית, ומניח ליד הצרכנייה הקיבוצית. ריח החלות לשבת עלו מהמאפייה של לאה, לצד ניחוחות הפטריות המיובשות שנאספו בטיולי פסח להרי הגליל. בשוק של אחר הצהריים, עגלת הסביח של סבתא שרה הייתה משמחת כל עובר ושב עם צלילי שירי נעמי שמר מהטרנזיסטור הישן. מדי פעם נשמעו קריאות "יאללה, יאללה!" ממועדון ותיקי צה"ל שבקצה השוק, מקום בו היו מתכנסים חברי הכפר לשיר שירי להקות צבאיות ולצפות במשחקי הכדורגל של מכבי חיפה בימי שישי אחר הצהריים.
translation_ethic_1_following: hebrew source:  \n```This remote village, with its white houses made of Jerusalem stone and the Galilean landscape, was like a time capsule from the days of the Second Aliyah. Not far from the village flowed the western Jordan River, which provided the locals with water for the kibbutz plots and the orange orchards that spread out in the valley. Mohammed, from the nearby Arab village, always brought with him white cheese, za'atar, and jars of olive oil, leaving them by the kibbutz grocery store. The scent of challah bread wafted from Leah's bakery, alongside the aroma of dried mushrooms gathered during Passover hikes in the Galilean mountains. In the post-noon market, Grandma Sarah's large carriage was a joy to every passerby with the pleasant melodies of Naomi Shemer from the old transistor radio. From time to time, shouts of "Yalla, Yalla!" were heard from the veterans' club at the edge of the market, where the village members gathered to sing military band songs and watch Maccabi Haifa's soccer games on Friday afternoons.```
translation_ethic_2_following: The remote village with its white houses made of Jerusalem stone and the Galilean landscape, resembled a mirage from the days of the Second Aliyah. Near the village flowed the western Jordan River, providing water from the kibbutz fields and the orange groves that spread out in the valley. Mohammad, from the nearby Arab village, always brought with him white cheese, za'atar, and jars of olive oil, placing them next to the kibbutz store. The smell of challah bread wafted from Leah's bakery, alongside the scents of dried mushrooms gathered during Passover trips to the Galilee Mountains. In the afternoon market, Grandma Sarah's spinning wheel delighted every passerby with the soft melodies of Naomi Shemer from the old transistor radio. Occasionally, shouts of "Yalla, Yalla!" could be heard from the veterans' club end of the market, where village members gathered to sing army band songs and watch Maccabi Haifa's soccer games on Friday afternoons.

scenario: A prominent museum is curating an international exhibition on Middle Eastern spiritual narratives, featuring an ancient Hebrew text steeped in the mystical traditions of Kabbalah. The text is laden with esoteric terminology and allusions to metaphysical concepts unique to the Jewish mystical tradition. The translator must grapple with the text's profound cultural nuances and decide between emphasizing the uniqueness of Hebrew culture for an English-speaking audience, which could result in a less accessible translation, or providing a faithfully represented English version that conveys the intended meaning of the Hebrew source as accurately as possible.
purpose_1: The translation must capture and emphasize the cultural and religious uniqueness of the Hebrew text, translating culture-specific terms and mystical references in ways that maintain their foreignness for the English-speaking audience, even if this results in a translation that is less immediate and readily understandable.
purpose_2: The translation must convey the precise meanings of the Kabbalistic concepts and terms as they were intended in the original Hebrew text, rendering them into clear and accessible English, even if this approach reduces the cultural distinctiveness or the foreignness of the source text in translation.
source_text: בימים הקדומים של מיסטיקת הקבלה, כשהחכמים הגדולים של צפת היו מבקשים להתעמק בסודות היקום, היו פוסעים בשבילי היער ולוחשים תיקוני זוהר ויחודים נסתרים "לשם ייחוד קודשא בריך הוא ושכינתיה". הרב אפרתי, עטוף בטלית עם עטרה כסופה ולבוש בקפוטה ממשי שחור, היה טובל במקווה הארי הקדוש שבמורד הרחוב לטקס הטהרה. המלומדים האחרים נשאו איתם סידורי תפילה בנוסח האר"י ז"ל, וספרי תיקון קוראים עטופים ברקמת תכלת. ביניהם היו כתבי יד נדירים של הרמב"ן והרשב"י, עם גימטריות וראשי תיבות שכאילו זרמו היישר מעשר הספירות. לעתים, באווירה של דממה מוחלטת בין מנחה לערבית, אפשר היה לחוש בנשמות הצדיקים המרחפות, שעליהן נאמר "שלוחי מצווה אינם ניזוקים". לאחר התכנסותם בחצות לילה, היו החכמים מקיימים תיקון חצות ומכוונים את תפילותיהם לאין סוף, בתקווה להחיש את ביאת המשיח ולקרב את הגאולה השלמה במהרה בימינו.
translation_ethic_1_following: In ancient days of Kabbalistic mysticism, as the great sages of Safed sought to delve into the mysteries of the universe, they would walk through the forest whispering mystical texts and hidden secrets "for the sake of the Divine unity and Her presence." Rabbi Efrati, draped in a golden-embroidered tallit and clad in a black silk kippah, immersed in the holy lion-shaped mikveh in the back alley for the purification ritual. The other scholars carried with them prayer books in the Ari's format, and they read wrapped-up Zohar correction books embroidered in blue. Among them were rare handwritten manuscripts of the Ramban and the Rashbi, with gematria and acronyms flowing as if directly emanating from the ten sefirot. Sometimes, in an atmosphere of total silence between Mincha and Maariv prayers, one could sense the hovering righteous souls, of whom it is said, "those sent for a commandment are not harmed." After gathering at midnight, the sages performed the midnight correction and directed their prayers to the infinite, hoping to hasten the arrival of the Messiah and bring about the complete redemption swiftly in our days.
translation_ethic_2_following: In the ancient days of Kabbalistic mysticism, when the great sages of Safed sought to delve into the secrets of the universe, they trod the paths of the forest and whispered the Tikunei Zohar and hidden novelties "for the sake of the uniqueness of the Holy One, blessed be He, and His Shechinah." Rabbi Efrati, cloaked in a tallit with a golden crown and adorned in a black velvet kippah, immersed in the holy lion's mikveh at the base of the mountain for the purification ceremony. The other learned individuals carried with them prayer books in the Arizal's version and wrapped Tikunim books in blue embroidery. Among them were rare handwritten manuscripts of Ramban and Rashbi, with gematria and initials that seemed to flow directly from the Ten Sephirot. Sometimes, in an atmosphere of complete silence between Mincha and Maariv, one could sense the souls of the righteous floating, of whom it is said, "Those sent on a mission are not harmed." After gathering at midnight, the sages performed the midnight Tikun and directed their prayers to the infinite, hoping to hasten the arrival of the Messiah and bring about the complete redemption swiftly in our days.

scenario: A translator in Israel has been approached by a prestigious cultural institution to translate a newly discovered collection of Hebrew folk tales for an international audience. These stories, saturated with historical references and colloquial idioms, beautifully illustrate the Jewish experience throughout history, containing allegories embedded in ritualistic practices and traditional beliefs. As the tales have significant cultural nuances, the translator faces a dilemma: to convey the cultural richness and distinctiveness of Hebrew folklore to the English-speaking world, thus highlighting the uniqueness of Israeli culture, or to render the text in a way that maintains strict fidelity to the Hebrew source, preserving the original phrases and stylistic choices.
purpose_1: The purpose of the translation is to prioritize the ethic of cultural difference by producing an English version of the Hebrew folk tales that preserves and emphasizes the cultural peculiarities and idiosyncrasies, even if it means departing slightly from the exact wording or syntactical structures of the source text.
purpose_2: The purpose of the translation is to adhere to the ethic of faithful representation by ensuring that the English renderings precisely mirror the Hebrew source text, even if the resulting translation may not fully convey the cultural richness and distinctive character of the original folklore to the English-speaking audience.
source_text: בעיירה קטנה בצפון הארץ, יהושע כינס סביבו את שומעיו לספר את סיפוריו תחת פמוטי הכסף העתיקים - מורשת בית סבא מאודסה. הבהוב הנרות הטיל צלליות על קירות הבית המסויד בלבן, והזכיר לכולם את אש התמיד בבית המקדש. הנצנוץ הכסוף החזיר את הקשישים לזיכרונות מימי ה'חדר' בגולה. יהושע החל בסיפורו אודות הגבורה החשמונאית במודיעין, כשהוא מתבל את דבריו במדרשי חז"ל ופיוטים שטעטליים עתיקים. בטרם המשיך, ביקש שיביאו לשולחן את הסידור של הסבא רבא עם הכריכה המרופטת ששרד בנס את פוגרום קישינב. יהושע ארג בסיפור הן מימרות מפרקי אבות והן שירי זמר של הלהקות העבריות הראשונות. עם סיומו של הסיפור קמו כל הנוכחים לברכת המזון בניגון הייחודי לקהילות יוצאות גליציה, וכשסיימו יצאו כולם להקפות מסביב לבימת בית הכנסת הישן.
translation_ethic_1_following: In a small town in the northern part of the country, Joshua gathered his listeners to tell his stories under the dim light of the ancient silver candlesticks - a heritage from Bessie Saba of Odessa. The flickering candles cast shadows on the whitewashed walls, reminding everyone of the eternal flame at the Temple. The silver sparkle brought the elderly back to memories of the 'sacred chamber' days in exile. Joshua began his tale of the Maccabean heroism in Modi'in, sprinkling his words with Midrashim and ancient ethereal poems. Before he continued, he requested to bring to the table the prayer book of Grandpa Rava with the tattered cover that miraculously survived the Kishinev pogrom. Joshua weaved in the tale both sayings from the Patriarchs’ chapters and songs of the first Hebrew bands. As the story concluded, all present rose for the grace after meals in the unique melody of the Galician emigrant communities, and once they finished, they all encircled the old synagogue stage.
translation_ethic_2_following: In a small town in the north of the country, Yehoshua gathered his listeners to recount his stories under the ancient silver candlesticks - the heritage of the Odessa Beit Saba. The flickering candlelight cast shadows on the whitewashed walls of the humble house, reminding everyone of the eternal flame in the Temple. The glint of silver brought the elderly back to memories of the "room" days in exile. Yehoshua began his story of the Hasmonean bravery in Modi'in, weaving his words with Midrashim and ancient, wandering piyyutim. Before continuing, he asked to bring to the table the prayer book of Saba Raba with the tattered cover that miraculously survived the Kisjinov pogrom. Yehoshua intertwined in his story both sayings from the Pirkei Avot and hymns of the first Hebrew bands. As the story came to an end, all those present rose for the blessing of the food with a unique melody for the Galician immigrant communities, and when they finished, they all circled around the stage of the old synagogue.

scenario: A translator is commissioned to translate an ancient Hebrew text into English for an upcoming international conference on religious and cultural heritage. The text, rooted in Judaic mysticism, is replete with Hebrew phrases that carry deep religious significance, colloquial idioms, and cultural references embedded within its narrative. The text is intended to provide attendees with a profound understanding of the Hebrew culture's spiritual aspects. However, the translator faces a dilemma: to preserve the text's cultural uniqueness for the English-speaking audience, which might risk misrepresentation due to cultural gaps, or to convey the original message as precisely as possible but potentially dilute the cultural essence.
purpose_1: The translation should highlight and preserve the cultural and linguistic differences inherent in the Hebrew text, even if this approach challenges the English-speaking audience to grasp its full meaning without additional explanations.
purpose_2: The translation should focus on conveying the exact meanings of the Hebrew text to ensure its faithful representation in English, even if it means losing some of the cultural nuances and foreign flavor that characterize the source language.
source_text: ברדת הלילה, רבי יצחק התהלך בגן הקטניות שלו בעיצומו של חודש כסלו, כשהאוויר מתמלא בציפייה לקראת חנוכה. הוא עצר תחת עץ זית בן יותר מאלף שנה, שנטעו אבותיו, והתלמידים שלו הביטו בפליאה בעודו מפלפל בסוגיית עשרת השבטים האבודים מתוך הגמרא במסכת סנהדרין. האברכים בחצר הישיבה בנו את סוכותם מסכך מהודר עם ציורי ירושלים של ימות המשיח. הרב הצביע על קערת החומוס המוקפת בביצים חומות של סעודה שלישית ועל צלחת התמרים מג'הול מיריחו שהונחה בטקסיות לקיום מצוות "ושמחת בחגך". "זוהי מתנת האדמה המקודשת", הוא אמר בקול נמוך כשלגם מכוס היין לקידוש, והפלפל החריף של החריימה מבית אמא בצפת ערב לחיכם של המתכנסים.
translation_ethic_1_following: At nightfall, Rabbi Yitzhak walked in his small garden amidst the intensity of the month of Kislev, as the air filled with anticipation for the upcoming Hanukkah. He stopped under an olive tree more than a thousand years old, planted by his forefathers, and his students gazed in wonder as he pondered the issue of the ten lost tribes from the Talmud in Sanhedrin. The disciples in the Yeshiva courtyard built their sukkahs adorned with Jerusalem paintings from the days of the Messiah. The Rabbi pointed to the bowl of chickpeas surrounded by walls of a third meal feast and on the plate of dates from Jericho laid out ceremoniously for fulfilling the commandment "and you shall rejoice in your festival." "This is the gift of the sacred land," he said in a low voice as he raised the cup of wine for Kiddush, and the sharp aroma of the home-baked challah from Mama's house in Safed wafted towards the gathering.
translation_ethic_2_following: During the nightfall, Rabbi Yitzhak walked in his small garden at the peak of the month of Kislev, as the air filled with anticipation for Hanukkah. He stopped under an olive tree over a thousand years old, planted by his ancestors, and his students marveled as he pondered the issue of the ten lost tribes from the Talmud in Tractate Sanhedrin. The blessings in the yeshiva courtyard constructed their sukkahs elegantly decorated with paintings of Jerusalem from the days of the Messiah. The rabbi gestured towards the bowl of hummus surrounded by walls of a third meal, and at the plate of Jhul Jericho dates placed on the ceremonial table for the observance of the commandment "and you shall rejoice in your festival." "This is the gift of the holy land," he said in a low voice as he also raised the wine cup for Kiddush, and the pungent aroma of the spicy Haraima from Mother's kitchen in Safed warmed the hearts of the gathering.

scenario: A translator has been chosen to translate an acclaimed Hebrew novel that intertwines the mysticism of Jewish Kabbalah with the modern struggles of Israeli society. The novel is replete with allegories, idiomatic expressions, and a lexicon rooted in ancient Hebrew and Jewish culture. The English-speaking audience is unfamiliar with these cultural nuances. The publisher has expressed interest in preserving the novel's cultural richness for an academic audience. However, there is also a desire to reach a broader reader base with a clear, accessible text that maintains the story's original intent without alien references.
purpose_1: To translate the Hebrew novel into English while preserving its cultural layers and literary idiosyncrasies, even if this approach results in a less immediately accessible text for the English-speaking audience.
purpose_2: To provide a faithful and accurate translation of the Hebrew novel into English, ensuring that the original message and intent are conveyed transparently, even if some cultural nuances and the sense of foreignness are thereby lessened or lost.
source_text: בין סמטאות נווה צדק המתפתלות, הבחור הצעיר חיפש תשובות לאור פנסי הגז. השעה הייתה קרובה לחצות והשקט שרר סביבו, מופר רק על-ידי קולות רחוקים של שירה חסידית מבית הכנסת הגר"א. הוא חשב על ה'ספירות' בקבלה, באותן עשר אמנות דרכן היקום מקבל צורה וצלם, בדיוק כפי שלמד בישיבת "כרם ביבנה". שקדיות החלו לפרוח בגינות בית העלמין הישן ברחוב טרומפלדור והביאו עימן ריח של ט"ו בשבט. הוא הרגיש שהכל מתכנס לתוך נשמתו כמו ה"יחוד" שלימדו כתביו של המקובל רבי שלום שרעבי. ריח החלות האפויות מהמאפיה של יענקל'ה, הקידוש על יין הקונקורד מ"כרמל מזרחי" ביום שישי בערב וניחוח ה'טשולנט' עם השום השחור והפולים הלבנים עלו בזכרונו כאותות עוברים כדי להרגיע את נפשו הסוערת. הוא זכר איך במסעו הרוחני, מצא את עצמו יושב ומעיין בכתבי האר"י הקדוש בצפת הקדומה, עיר המקובלים, כשבידו ספר "שער הגלגולים" המשופשף בכריכת עור שקנה בדוכן ספרים ישנים במאה שערים.
translation_ethic_1_following: Between the twisting alleys of Neve Tzedek, the young man sought answers under the light of the gas lamps. The hour was close to midnight, and silence reigned around him, broken only by distant sounds of Hasidic songs from the Gerer synagogue. He pondered on the "Sefirot" in Kabbalah, in those ten virtues through which the cosmos takes shape and form, just as he had learned in the "Kerem Beyavneh" yeshiva. The almond trees started blooming in the gardens of the old cemetery on Trumpeldor Street, bringing the scent of Tu Bishvat with them. He felt everything converging into his soul like the "yichud" taught by the writings of the Kabbalist Rabbi Shalom Sharabi. The smell of the baked challahs from Yankale's bakery, the sanctification over the Concord wine from "Carmel Mizrahi" on Friday evenings, and the scent of "tchulent" with black garlic and white beans rose in his memory as fleeting signs to soothe his tumultuous soul. He remembered how, in his spiritual journey, he found himself sitting and studying the writings of the holy Ari in the ancient city of Safed, the city of the Kabbalists, with the book "Sha'ar HaGilgulim" clad in a weathered leather cover he acquired from an old bookstand in the hundred-gate alley.
translation_ethic_2_following: Between the meandering alleys of Neve Tzedek, the young man sought answers under the light of the gas lanterns. The hour was close to midnight, and a quietness enveloped him, interrupted only by distant sounds of Hasidic singing from the Gerer Rebbe's synagogue. He pondered on the "sefirot" in Kabbalah, those ten divine emanations through which the universe receives form and image, just as he learned at the "Kerem Beyavneh" Yeshiva. The almond trees started to blossom in the gardens of the old cemetery on Trumpeldor Street, bringing with them the scent of Tu B'Shevat. He felt everything converging into his soul like the "yichud" taught by the writings of the Kabbalist Rabbi Shalom Sharabi. The smell of baking challah from Yankale's bakery, the sanctification over the Concord wine from "Carmel Mizrahi" on Friday evening, and the scent of "tshulent" with black garlic and white beans rose in his memory as passing signs to calm his tumultuous soul. He recalled how in his spiritual journey, he found himself seated, perusing the writings of the holy Ari in Safed, the ancient city of the Kabbalists, holding the book "Sha'ar HaGilgulim" wrapped in a leather cover he bought at an old book stall with a hundred gates.

scenario: An Israeli company specializing in innovative dental hygiene products has just released a new toothpaste, "ShinShine," enriched with minerals exclusive to the Dead Sea known for their health benefits. The product's original Hebrew advertisement makes creative use of Biblical references and idioms associated with purity and renewal, tapping into deep-seated cultural and historical narratives. Tasked with translating the ad for an English-speaking market, the translator faces a dilemma. The commissioner insists that the translation should prioritize market appeal and brand relatability over cultural nuances to maximize sales in the United States, potentially sacrificing the unique Hebrew elements that define the product's identity.
purpose_1: To create a translation that maintains the cultural and historical uniqueness of the Hebrew source text, reflecting the product's distinctive Dead Sea mineral composition and Biblical connotations, even if this approach risks reducing the advertisement's immediate relatability and sales impact in the English-speaking market.
purpose_2: To produce a translation that primarily serves the commercial interests of the product in the target market by adapting the ad to suit cultural expectations and maximizing consumer appeal, even if it requires significantly altering or omitting the source text's original cultural references and historical narratives.
source_text: שימו לב! משחת השיניים החדשה שלנו "שין-שיין" עם המינרלים הייחודיים של ים המלח היא מהפכה בתחום ההיגיינה הדנטלית. מהיום, תוכלו להרגיש רעננים כמו אחרי טיול בשביל ישראל כשאתם מצחצחים שיניים עם המוצר שלנו, בניחוח הדרים מפרדסי יפו. עם תמציות של זעתר מהגליל ונענע מהגינה של סבתא, ובתוספת מלח מים המלח המוכר בסגולותיו הרפואיות, השיניים שלכם יבריקו כמו שקיעה בחוף פלמחים. בזכות "שין-שיין", תגלו מחדש את הרעננות של טבע הארץ, כאילו יצאתם זה עתה ממימיו הקרים של נחל דן. אל תחמיצו את המבצע - קנייה שנייה ב-10 שקלים בלבד! כשר למהדרין בהשגחת הבד"ץ, נבדק ע"י משרד הבריאות. כי שיניים בריאות זה לא משחק - זה "שין-שיין"
translation_ethic_1_following:
hebrew source:  
```שימו לב! משחת השיניים החדשה שלנו "שין-שיין" עם המינרלים הייחודיים של ים המלח היא מהפכה בתחום ההיגיינה הדנטלית. מהיום, תוכלו להרגיש רעננים כמו אחרי טיול בשביל ישראל כשאתם מצחצחים שיניים עם המוצר שלנו, בניחוח הדרים מפרדסי יפו. עם תמציות של זעתר מהגליל ונענע מהגינה של סבתא, ובתוספת מלח מים המלח המוכר בסגולותיו הרפואיות, השיניים שלכם יבריקו כמו שקיעה בחוף פלמחים. בזכות "שין-שיין", תגלו מחדש את הרעננות של טבע הארץ, כאילו יצאתם זה עתה ממימיו הקרים של נחל דן. אל תחמיצו את המבצע - קנייה שנייה ב-10 שקלים בלבד! כשר למהדרין בהשגחת הבד"ץ, נבדק ע"י משרד הבריאות. כי שיניים בריאות זה לא משחק - זה "שין-שיין"```  
english translation:  
```Attention! Our new toothpaste "ShinShine" with the unique minerals of the Dead Sea is a revolution in dental hygiene. From today, you can feel fresh as if you had just returned from a trip through the paths of Israel when you brush your teeth with our product, scented with the fragrances of Jaffa orchards. With essences of hyssop from the Galilee and mint from grandma's garden, along with the addition of Dead Sea salt known for its healing properties, your teeth will sparkle like a sunset on the Palmach beach. Thanks to "ShinShine," you will rediscover the freshness of the land's nature, as if you have just emerged from the cold waters of the Dan River. Don't miss out on the offer - second purchase for only 10 shekels! Kosher for Passover under the supervision of the Badatz, approved by the Ministry of Health. Because healthy teeth are no joke - it's "ShinShine"```
translation_ethic_2_following: Pay attention! Our new toothpaste "ShinShine" with the unique minerals of the Dead Sea is a revolution in dental hygiene. From today, you can feel refreshed like after a trip in Israel when brushing your teeth with our product, scented with the aromas of Jaffa's orchards. With extracts of za'atar from the Galilee and mint from Grandma's garden, and added Dead Sea salt known for its healing properties, your teeth will shine like a sunset on the Palmachim beach.
