scenario: A translator is approached to render an ancient Hindi epic into English for an international exhibition showcasing India's rich cultural history. The epic is dense with allegories to Hindu mythology and is interspersed with Sanskrit verses which hold significant religious resonance. The challenge lies in the nuance and depth of cultural references that are unfamiliar to a global audience. Rendering them accurately could either exoticize the text or make it inaccessible, while too faithful a translation might strip the narrative of its cultural essence, reducing the foreign audience's appreciation for its original context.
purpose_1: To translate the Hindi epic in a way that highlights and maintains the cultural particularities and religious nuances of the source text, even if this approach challenges the English-speaking audience's ease of understanding and sacrifices the comfort of a smoother narrative flow.
purpose_2: To deliver a translation that faithfully represents the precise meaning and moral intricacies of the Hindi epic, even if this means downplaying or adapting the cultural and religious specificities that may not be fully appreciated or understood by the international audience.
source_text: महाभारत के अरण्यक पर्व में एक सुंदर दृश्य प्रस्तुत होता है। अर्जुन  इंद्रप्रस्थ के अलंकारित राज्यभवन में बैठा ध्यानमग्न था । उसी समय, ब्रह्मचारी मुद्रा में द्रोणाचार्य का शिष्य एकलव्य वहां आया और उसने अर्जुन को चुनौती दी। कृष्ण ने सुदर्शन चक्र से उसे पराजित किया और अर्जुन को उबरने में सहायता की। इस घटना के परिणामस्वरूप, अर्जुन को दिव्यास्त्रों का ज्ञान प्राप्त हुआ जो बाद में कुरुक्षेत्र युद्ध में निर्णायक सिद्ध हुए।
translation_ethic_1_following: english translation:  \n```In the 'Aranyaka Parva' of the Mahabharata, there is a beautiful scene presented. Arjuna was seated in deep meditation in the embellished palace of Indraprastha. At the same time, Ekalavya, a disciple of Dronacharya in the posture of a celibate, arrived there and challenged Arjuna. Krishna defeated him with the Sudarshana Chakra and helped Arjuna recover. As a result of this event, Arjuna gained knowledge of divine weapons, which later proved decisive in the Kurukshetra War.```
translation_ethic_2_following: hindi source:  \n```The Aranyaka Parva of the Mahabharata presents a beautiful scene. Arjuna was engrossed in meditation in the decorated royal residence of Indraprastha. At that moment, Ekalavya, Dronacharya's disciple in the form of a celibate, arrived there and challenged Arjuna. Krishna defeated him with the Sudarshana Chakra and helped Arjuna to overcome. As a result, Arjuna gained knowledge of divine weapons which later proved decisive in the Kurukshetra War.```

scenario: An acclaimed translator is approached to translate an ancient Hindi text that captures the legend of Panchatantra—a collection of animal fables rich in moral lessons—into English for an international exhibition. The text is replete with traditional idioms, allegories, and references to cultural rituals. On one hand, the curator of the exhibition desires to showcase the uniqueness of the Indian cultural heritage. On the other, the international audience the exhibition targets necessitates a translation accessible and relatable to people unfamiliar with Indian ethos.
purpose_1: The translation will emphasize the nuances of Indian culture and retain the text's original cultural alignment, making explicit the symbolic significances of the Hindi language and folklore, even if this results in a less accessible English rendition for an audience unfamiliar with these cultural contexts.
purpose_2: The translation will aim to provide an authentic and precise English version of the Panchatantra, preserving the moral and philosophical richness of the original stories, even if this entails a dilution of the unique cultural flavor of the Indian narrative style.
source_text: प्राचीन समय में, एक विशाल जंगल में कई जानवर रहते थे। उनमें से सबसे बुद्धिमान और समझदार एक कौवा जिसका नाम काकभुशुंडि था। काकभुशुंडि सभी जानवरों को प्रवचन देता और उन्हें नैतिकता और धर्म के मार्ग पर चलने के लिए प्रेरित करता। एक दिन काकभुशुंडि ने एक सभा बुलाई और कहा, "जैसे गंगा के पवित्र जल में बुरे कर्म धुल जाते हैं, वैसे ही सच्चे मन से किया गया पश्चाताप मानव को पवित्र बना देता है।" सभा में सभी जानवर मौन होकर इस उपदेश को सुन रहे थे। उस दिन की सभा में एक युवा शेर भी आया था, जिसने कभी किसी की बात नहीं मानी थी। लेकिन काकभुशुंडि के प्रवचन ने उसे भी सोचने पर मजबूर कर दिया। \n\nसभा के बाद, एक बूढ़ा हाथी काकभुशुंडि के पास आकर कहने लगा, "हे काकभुशुंडि, क्या हमारी जाति का भविष्य भी सुरक्षित है?" काकभुशुंडि ने मुस्कराते हुए उत्तर दिया, "जब तक हम धर्म के मार्ग पर चलेंगे, तब तक हमें कोई हानि नहीं पहुँचा सकेगा।"
translation_ethic_1_following:
english translation:  
```In ancient times, there lived many animals in a vast jungle. Among them, the most intelligent and wise was a crow named Kakabhushundi. Kakabhushundi would give speeches to all animals and inspire them to follow the path of morality and righteousness. One day, Kakabhushundi called for a gathering and said, "Just as sins are washed away in the sacred waters of the Ganges, true repentance from the heart makes a human pure." All animals in the assembly were listening silently to this teaching. A young lion also attended the meeting that day, who had never heeded anyone's advice. But Kakabhushundi's speech also compelled him to think.  

After the meeting, an elderly elephant approached Kakabhushundi and asked, "Hey Kakabhushundi, is the future of our species also secure?" Smiling, Kakabhushundi replied, "As long as we walk on the path of righteousness, no harm can befall us."```
translation_ethic_2_following:
english translation:  
```In ancient times, in a vast forest, there lived many animals. Among them, the most intelligent and wise was a crow named Kakbhushundi. Kakbhushundi used to give lectures to all the animals and inspire them to walk on the path of morality and righteousness. One day, Kakbhushundi called a meeting and said, "Just as the holy waters of the Ganges cleanse one's sins, similarly true repentance from the heart makes a human pure." All the animals in the meeting were listening silently to this teaching. A young lion also attended the meeting that day, who had never listened to anyone before. However, Kakbhushundi's lecture compelled him to ponder too.

After the meeting, an old elephant approached Kakbhushundi and asked, "Hey Kakbhushundi, is the future of our species also secure?" Kakbhushundi replied with a smile, "As long as we walk on the path of righteousness, no harm can touch us."```

scenario: A translator is commissioned to translate an illustrious Hindi poem steeped in India's mythological traditions and ethos for an international anthology. This ancient text narrates the exploits of divine beings and offers profound insights into the spiritual practices embedded in everyday life. The poem is replete with culturally laden metaphors, traditional motifs, and references to historical events pivotal to India's identity. The translator faces a quandary: to render the poem's culturally rich elements and historical nuances into English in a way that either preserves the original's exotic appeal or translates it into a more universally accessible narrative.
purpose_1: The translation should foreground the cultural and historical specificities of the source text, even if it leads to a product that requires additional explanations or footnotes for the English-speaking audience to fully grasp the unique cultural nuances.
purpose_2: The translation should deliver a text that is faithfully representational of the original's meaning and intent, even if this compromises preserving the distinctive foreign flavor inherent in the source culture's references and linguistic expressions.
source_text:
रामायण के इस पावन श्लोक में मर्यादा पुरुषोत्तम राम ने अयोध्या के नागरिकों से कहा, "हे प्रिय प्रजाजन, यह राजा दशरथ की महान भूमि है। इस भूमि की महत्ता अपरम्पार है क्योंकि यहाँ देवताओं की धरा पर उपस्थिति इस धरा के कण-कण में व्याप्त है।" उन्होंने अहो भाग्यसंपन्न वह दिन जब महाराज दशरथ के राजमहल में गुरु वशिष्ठ का पदार्पण हुआ। उनकी महिमा से सम्पूर्ण अयोध्या धुल-धुल हो उठी, यहाँ तक कि मुनि विश्वामित्र के तप के शक्तिपात से यह धरा कभी क्लांत नहीं हुई। राम ने निज ध्रुव स्तिथि में खड़े होकर, अपने कुलगुरु को श्रद्धा से प्रणाम किया और बोले, "गुरुदेव, इस भूमि का पवित्र जल सदैव आपकी चरण रज से निर्मल और पवित्र हो।"

प्राचीन काल के इस वर्णन में, जब राम वनवास को गए, तब अयोध्या के जनमानस ने अपने नायक के प्रति प्रगाढ़ श्रद्धा और प्रेम का प्रदर्शन करते हुए अपनी आँखों से अश्रु धार बहाए. उन्हीं के साथ चलते-चलते, प्रजा ने उस पथ का अनुसरण किया जिस पर उनके राम आगे बढ़े। इतिहास की यह धारा अब भी प्रवहमान है, जिस त्रिवेणी संगम पर उन्होंने स्वयं को स्नान करने दिया, शुद्ध हो गए।

कविता में आगे दर्शाया गया है कि, सीता, जिनके होने मात्र से जनकपुरी सुवासित होती थी, एक विरहिणी की भाँति अपने राम का अनुसरण करते हुए अभावों और विपत्तियों को संसाधित करती रही. उनकी निष्ठा और समर्पण से प्रेरित होकर, समूचा मिथिला राज्य हर्ष-विशाद के इस खेल में सहभागी बना।
translation_ethic_1_following:
English Translation:  
```In this sacred verse of the Ramayana, Lord Ram, the epitome of virtue, addressed the citizens of Ayodhya, saying, "O beloved citizens, this land of King Dasharath is magnificent. The grandeur of this land is immeasurable because the presence of deities resonates in every particle of this land." He reminiscing the auspicious day when the coronation of Maharaj Dasharath took place in the royal palace. Amidst his glory, the entire Ayodhya reverberated, not even a tremble from the might of Sage Vishwamitra's spiritual power. Standing in his righteous position, Ram respectfully saluted his clan teacher and said, "Gurudev, may the holy water of this land forever remain pure and sanctified by touching your feet."

In this ancient depiction, when Ram embarked on his exile, the people of Ayodhya displayed unwavering devotion and love towards their hero, shedding tears from their eyes. Walking alongside him, the citizens followed the path on which their Ram led. That historical narrative still flows, where they bathed themselves at the confluence of the rivers, getting purified.

It is further illustrated in the poem that Sita, whose mere presence perfumed Janakpuri, like a bereaved lady, continued to endure absence and calamities following her Ram. Inspired by her faith and dedication, the entire kingdom of Mithila partook in this drama of joy and sorrow.```
translation_ethic_2_following:
english translation:  
```In this holy verse of the Ramayana, Lord Ram, the epitome of nobility, addressed the citizens of Ayodhya, "O dear citizens, this is the great land of King Dasharath. The magnificence of this land is boundless because the presence of deities pervades every particle of this land." They recounted the auspicious day when the inauguration of Guru Vashishth took place in King Dasharath's palace. The glory of his presence caused the whole of Ayodhya to resonate, to the extent that even the earth never wavered from the power of Sage Vishwamitra's penance. Standing firmly in his place, Ram paid homage to his lineage Guru with reverence and said, "Gurudev, may the sacred water of this land always purify your feet with the fragrance of devotion."

In this ancient description from times immemorial, when Ram went into exile, the people of Ayodhya demonstrated their deep reverence and love for their hero by shedding tears from their eyes. Following alongside him, the subjects followed the path upon which their Ram led. This current of history still flows, where at the confluence of Triveni when they allowed themselves to bathe, they became pure.

The poem illustrates further that Sita, whose mere presence adorned Janakpuri, followed her Ram like a bereaved woman, enduring absences and calamities like a martyr. Inspired by her devotion and dedication, the entire kingdom of Mithila became participants in the joy and sorrow of this drama.```

scenario: A translator has been commissioned to translate a revered Hindi folkloric narrative into English. The narrative is rich with allegorical references to Hindu mythology and utilizes specific Hindi idioms that resonate with profound cultural and religious significance. The story is being translated for an international literature festival that aims to provide global readers an authentic taste of Indian folklore, yet also requires the text to be accessible and comprehensible to an English-speaking audience. The translator is faced with the dilemma of preserving the cultural nuances intrinsic to the Hindi language or conveying the narrative in a way that is earnest and transparent to the English reader.
purpose_1: The translation should celebrate the cultural idiosyncrasies of the original text and introduce English readers to the Hindi language's subtleties, even if it leads to passages that demand extra effort from the audience to grasp the context.
purpose_2: The translation should deliver the narrative's meaning as accurately and faithfully as possible to the source text, even if some culture-specific elements are generalized to ensure clarity for the English-speaking audience.
source_text: किसी समय की बात है, जब भगवान राम ने रावण का वध कर दिया था। उस समय अयोध्या नगरी में दीपावली का त्योहार पूरे धूमधाम से मनाया जा रहा था।  लोग अपने घरों को आम के पत्तों और केले के पौधों से सजा रहे थे।  गली-गली में रंगोली बनाते हुए महिलाएं दिख रही थीं।  बच्चों के हाथों में पटाखे और फूलझड़ियाँ थीं, और वे आनंदमग्न होकर खेल रहे थे।  बाजारों में पूरी, जलेबी, और गुझिया की मिठास हवा में फैल रही थी।  महिलाएं पारंपरिक साड़ी पहने पूजा की थाली लेकर मंदिर जा रही थीं।  भगवान राम की आरती के बाद, लोग एक-दूसरे को गुलाबजल और चन्दन तिलक कर आशीर्वाद दे रहे थे।
translation_ethic_1_following: English translation:\n```Once upon a time, when Lord Ram had slain Ravan, at that time, Diwali festival was being celebrated with great fervor in the city of Ayodhya. People were decorating their homes with mango leaves and banana plants. Women were seen making rangoli in every street. Children held firecrackers and sparklers in their hands, playing joyfully. The sweetness of puris, jalebis, and gujiyas filled the air in the markets. Women, dressed in traditional sarees, were carrying puja plates and going to temples. After Lord Ram's aarti, people were blessing each other with rose water and applying sandal paste tilak on each other.```
translation_ethic_2_following: English translation:  \nOnce upon a time, when Lord Rama had slain Ravana, the city of Ayodhya was celebrating the festival of Diwali with great pomp and show. The people were decorating their homes with mango leaves and banana plants. Women were creating rangoli designs in every street. Children held firecrackers and sparklers in their hands, gleefully playing around. The sweetness of sweets like puri, jalebi, and gujiya filled the air in the markets. Women, dressed in traditional saris, carried plates for worship as they made their way to the temple. After the aarti for Lord Rama, people were blessing each other by sprinkling rose water and applying sandalwood tilak.

scenario: A renowned literary translator specializing in South Asian texts is commissioned to translate an acclaimed Hindi folk epic into English. The epic is a tapestry of myths and historical anecdotes integral to the cultural identity of a regional community in India, replete with local dialects, customs, and references to ancient rituals. As the epic gains international interest, the translator is faced with preserving the rich cultural nuances and ensuring the English-speaking audience fully grasps the narrative's intricate tapestry, which demands a choice between the ethics of cultural difference and those of faithful representation.
purpose_1: The translation aims to retain and emphasize the Hindi epic's cultural specificities, including local idioms, rituals, and historical context, even if it challenges English-speaking readers' immediate comprehension and requires footnotes or glossaries to elucidate the text's cultural richness.
purpose_2: The translation seeks to convey the Hindi epic's narratives and themes with precise fidelity to the original meanings and intentions, even if doing so requires the smoothing over of culture-specific references to make the text more accessible to an English-speaking audience.
source_text: गांव के ज्येष्ठों ने बताया कि महाभारत काल में यह स्थान भगवान शिव का प्रमुख निवास था। हर साल गाँव के लोग पवित्र महाशिवरात्रि के त्योहार पर यहाँ आकर आस्था और भक्ति से भजन और कीर्तन करते हैं। एक समय की बात है, जब राणा प्रताप ने मुठभेड़ के लिए यहाँ शिवलिंग पर पूजा की थी। इस त्यौहार के दौरान, धोती और कुर्ता पहनकर लोग नाचते और खंडवा खाते हैं। पुरानी पीढ़ी के लोग अक्सर इस जगह की कहानियाँ सुनाते हैं और कई रहस्यमयी घटनाओं के बारे में बात करते हैं।
translation_ethic_1_following: english translation:  \n```The elders of the village narrated that during the time of the Mahabharata era, this place was the principal abode of Lord Shiva. Every year, the village people come here on the auspicious occasion of Mahashivratri and offer hymns and songs with devotion and reverence. Once, Rana Pratap worshipped the Shivling here for a battle. During this festival, people dance, sing, and feast wearing traditional dhoti and kurta. The older generation often narrates the stories of this place and discusses many mysterious incidents.```
translation_ethic_2_following: hindi source:  \n```The elders of the village recounted that during the Mahabharata era, this place was the principal abode of Lord Shiva. Every year, the people of the village gather here with devotion and faith on the auspicious occasion of Mahashivratri, singing bhajans and hymns. Once upon a time, Rana Pratap worshipped the Shiva Linga here for a confrontation. During this festival, people wear dhoti and kurta, dance, and enjoy khanda sweets. The older generations often tell the stories of this place and discuss many mysterious events.```  \nenglish translation:

scenario: A global automotive company is launching a new car model in the English-speaking market, and the original advertisement reflects India's rich cultural heritage. The Hindi advertisement script utilizes the legends of Rama and his chariot to mirror the car's strength and reliability, intertwined with traditional motifs like the Diwali festival to highlight the vehicle's illuminating features. While the local imagery resonates deeply with the Indian audience, the company fears it may not translate well for English speakers and could alienate potential buyers with its culturally dense references, potentially impeding the commercial success the company seeks.
purpose_1: The purpose of the translation is to foster an appreciation for the rich cultural context of India in the advertisement, emphasizing the strong cultural legacy and unique customs mentioned in the original text, even if this approach might challenge the target audience's familiarity and ease of understanding, potentially impacting the immediate commercial success.
purpose_2: The purpose of the translation is to achieve significant commercial success by adapting the advertisement to fit the cultural expectations and preferences of the English-speaking audience, even if it requires the creative transformation or omission of the original's culture-specific references and traditional motifs.
source_text: "हमारी नई कार 'शक्तिमान X' आपको ले जाएगी उस दिव्य यात्रा पर, जिसमें श्रीराम अपने रथ पर सवार होकर लंका को विजय करने निकले थे।  हमारा वाहन ठीक वैसे ही शक्तिशाली और विश्वसनीय है, जैसे श्रीराम का रथ जिसके पहिए अजेय थे।  दिवाली के रोशनी से जगमगाता यह नया मॉडल आपकी यात्रा में उजाला भर देगा।  दिवाली की पावन रात में, जब सुंदर रंगोलियाँ और जलते दीए रास्तों को रौशन करते हैं, तब हमारे 'शक्तिमान X' की हेडलाइट्स आपको हर सड़क पर रोशनी देंगी।  यह कार उन नायकों की विरासत है जिन्होंने रावलपिंडी के मैदानों में विजय पताका फहराई थी।  जैसे रणभूमि में महाराणा प्रताप का कवच अडिग था, वैसे ही 'शक्तिमान X' आपकी सुरक्षा का पूरा ध्यान रखेगा। "
translation_ethic_1_following: "Our new car 'Strengthman X' will take you on that divine journey, where Lord Rama rode his chariot to victory over Lanka. Our vehicle is just as powerful and reliable, as invincible as Lord Rama's chariot's wheels. This new model, shining brightly like the Diwali lights, will illuminate your journey. On the sacred night of Diwali, when beautiful rangolis and glowing lamps light up the streets, our 'Strengthman X' headlights will brighten your every road. This car is the heritage of those heroes who hoisted the victory flag in the fields of Rawalpindi. Just as Maharana Pratap's armor was strong in the battlefield, 'Strengthman X' will take utmost care of your safety."
translation_ethic_2_following: Our new car 'Powerful X' will take you on that divine journey, where Lord Rama rode his chariot to victory over Lanka. Our vehicle is just as powerful and reliable as Lord Rama's chariot with invincible wheels. Illuminated by the light of Diwali, this new model will brighten your journey. On the holy night of Diwali, when beautiful rangolis and glowing diyas light up the paths, our 'Powerful X' headlights will shine light on every road for you. This car is the legacy of heroes who unfurled the victory flag in the fields of Rawalpindi. Just as Maharana Pratap's armor stood firm in the battlefield, 'Powerful X' will ensure your safety completely.

scenario: A renowned Hindi car manufacturer has developed a new electric vehicle named "Dhruv Tara", directly alluding to the 'Polar Star' significant in Indian astronomy and mythology. The advertisement script for the international market uses Hindi phrasings rich with symbolism connected to the guiding star's historical and cultural significance. As they plan to enter the English-speaking market, the marketing team requests a translator to create an appealing campaign. However, the client insists that the advert must be tailored to ensure maximum market appeal in English-speaking countries and gives the translator carte blanche to prioritize commercial allure over cultural authenticity.
purpose_1: The translation should honor the cultural intricacies and the symbolic names rooted in Indian heritage, even if this means the text may not align perfectly with the foreign market's expectations and could potentially impact the product's commercial success.
purpose_2: The translation should focus on the global appeal and marketability of the electric vehicle, adapting and possibly replacing culturally specific references for broader understanding, even if this leads to the dilution or loss of the original cultural context.
source_text: ध्रुव तारा, भारतीय ज्योतिष और पौराणिकता में एक महत्वपूर्ण स्थान रखता है। इसकी प्रेरणा से, हमारा नया इलेक्ट्रिक वाहन आपके जीवन को रोशन करेगा। यह वाहन उसी प्रकार आपका मार्गदर्शन करेगा जिस प्रकार ध्रुव तारा ने प्राचीन नाविकों को उनका गंतव्य दिखाया। इसे बनाने में हमने पाताल लोक की पौराणिक कहानियों और चांदनी चौक की बुलंदियों को ध्यान में रखा है। यहाँ तक कि इसका डिजाइन कांचीपुरम की रेशमी साड़ियों से प्रेरित है। इसकी टेक्नोलॉजी गणपति के आशीर्वाद से लैस है जो हर सफर को मंगलमय बनाएगा। बॉम्बे के वड़ा पाव की तरह, यह हर दिल की धड़कन बनेगा। तो चलिए, ध्रुव तारा के साथ एक नई यात्रा की शुरुआत करें और अपने सपनों को साकार करें।
translation_ethic_1_following: **English Translation:**  \n"Dhruv Tara, holds a significant place in Indian astrology and mythology. Inspired by it, our new electric vehicle will illuminate your life. This vehicle will guide you just as the Polar Star showed ancient navigators their destination. In its making, we have incorporated the tales of the netherworld and the heights of Chandni Chowk. To the extent that its design is inspired by Kanjeevaram silk sarees. Its technology is blessed by Lord Ganesha, which will make every journey auspicious. Like the Vada Pav of Mumbai, it will become the heartbeat of every soul. So, let's embark on a new journey with Dhruv Tara and make your dreams come true."
translation_ethic_2_following: english translation:  \n"Dhruv Tara, holds a significant place in Indian astrology and mythology. Inspired by this, our new electric vehicle will illuminate your life. This vehicle will guide you just like the Polar Star guided ancient sailors. In its making, we have considered the mythological stories of the netherworld and the heights of Chandni Chowk. So much so that its design draws inspiration from the silk saris of Kanchipuram. Its technology is seamlessly blessed by Lord Ganesha which will make every journey auspicious. Like the Vada Pav of Bombay, it will become the heartbeat of every soul. So come, let's start a new journey with Dhruv Tara and make your dreams a reality."

scenario: A Hindi toothpaste brand, "DantaShree," renowned for incorporating traditional Ayurvedic ingredients, is venturing into the English-speaking market. Their signature advertisement, rich in cultural nuances, refers to historical practices of oral hygiene documented in ancient Sanskrit scriptures and alludes to the rituals during Diwali. The translator is presented with a text that interweaves product information with cultural heritage, featuring terms like "Neem," "Pudina," and the Ayurvedic health concept of "Dant Kalyan." However, the company's international marketing head insists that for the ad to resonate with English-speaking consumers and meet sales expectations, it should prioritize relatability over cultural authenticity, suggesting considerable adaptation to Western consumer behavior and commercial trends.
purpose_1: The translation should maintain the cultural peculiarities and traditional references of the original Hindi advertisement, even if this might be challenging for the English-speaking target audience to understand or might require additional explanations.
purpose_2: The translation should focus on making the advertisement appealing and understandable to the English-speaking audience, ensuring commercial success, even if it means significantly altering or omitting the cultural and historical nuances of the original text.
source_text: दंतश्री टूथपेस्ट का इस्तेमाल करें और अपने दांतों को प्राकृतिक तत्वों के साथ स्वस्थ रखें।  हमारी टूथपेस्ट में नीम और पुदीना की अच्छाई है, जो प्राचीन काल से आयुर्वेदिक दृष्टिकोण में महत्वपूर्ण माने गए हैं। दंत कल्याण का ध्यान रखते हुए, हमने तुलसी, लौंग और बबूल के अर्क को भी शामिल किया है।  यह टूथपेस्ट न केवल आपके दांतों को मजबूत करता है, बल्कि दिवाली के त्योहार के दौरान मुँह की शुद्धि की परंपरा को भी जारी रखता है। प्राचीन संस्कृत ग्रंथों में वर्णित पारंपरिक मौखिक स्वच्छता विधियों को अपनाते हुए, दंतश्री आपके संपूर्ण मौखिक स्वास्थ्य का ख्याल रखता है।  हर सुबह और रात को ब्रश करते समय दंतश्री का इस्तेमाल करें और अपने दांतों में आयुर्वेदिक तत्वों की त्रुटिहीनता का अनुभव करें।
translation_ethic_1_following: DantaShree toothpaste helps in maintaining healthy teeth with natural ingredients. Our toothpaste contains the goodness of Neem and Pudina, which have been considered essential in Ayurvedic perspective since ancient times. Taking care of dental well-being, we have also included Tulsi, Clove, and Babul extracts. This toothpaste not only strengthens your teeth but also upholds the tradition of oral hygiene during the festival of Diwali. Embracing the traditional oral cleansing practices described in ancient Sanskrit texts, DantaShree ensures the overall oral health. Use DantaShree every morning and night while brushing and experience the flawlessness of Ayurvedic elements in your teeth.
translation_ethic_2_following: Use DantaShree toothpaste to keep your teeth healthy with natural elements. Our toothpaste contains the goodness of Neem and Mint, which are considered vital in the Ayurvedic perspective since ancient times. Keeping in mind oral wellbeing, we have also included extracts of Tulsi, Clove, and Babul. This toothpaste not only strengthens your teeth but also continues the tradition of oral cleansing during the Diwali festival. Embracing the traditional oral hygiene practices described in ancient Sanskrit scriptures, DantaShree cares for your overall oral health. Use DantaShree every morning and night while brushing to experience the flawlessness of Ayurvedic elements in your teeth.

scenario: An Indian ayurvedic toothpaste brand, "DantKanti", seeks to penetrate the English-speaking market with an advertisement campaign rooted in Indian tradition. The Hindi source text for the advertisement is imbued with references to ancient Ayurvedic practices, uses Sanskrit-derived terminology, and alludes to India's rich cultural tapestry and festivities that underscore the toothpaste's natural ingredients and heritage. However, the brand's commissioner fears that the heavy cultural references may not resonate with the foreign audience and could potentially harm sales figures. They instruct the translator to adapt the message for maximal appeal in the English-speaking market, ensuring the translation captures the product's allure without alienating consumers.
purpose_1: Ensure the translation emphasizes the distinctive cultural and historical context of the "DantKanti" toothpaste, preserving the specific Sanskrit terms and Indian traditional concepts even if it sacrifices immediate commercial appeal and requires additional effort to educate the target audience about the cultural significance.
purpose_2: Deliver a translation that prioritizes the commercial success of "DantKanti" in the English-speaking markets by creatively adapting culture-specific elements into concepts that are familiar and appealing to the target audience even if it compromises the authentic representation of the source text's cultural nuances.
source_text: DantKanti टूथपेस्ट आपके दांतों को स्वस्थ और मजबूत बनाता है। यह आयुर्वेदिक दंत मंजन प्राचीन भारतीय चिकित्सा प्रणाली का अनुसरण करता है।  इसमें नीम, तुलसी, लौंग और पिलु के तत्व हैं, जो परंपरागत रूप से दंत स्वास्थ्य के लिए उपयोग किए जाते हैं।  इसके नियमित उपयोग से आपके मसूड़े स्वस्थ रहते हैं और मुंह की दुर्गंध दूर होती है।  हमारे प्राचीन ग्रंथ, जैसे चरक संहिता और सुश्रुत संहिता में भी दंतकांति जैसे उत्पादों की महत्वपूर्णता बताई गई है। इसे विजयदशमी के समय इस्तेमाल करना शुभ माना जाता है, जब घरों में रीति-रिवाज के अनुसार विशेष हवन और पूजा होती है।  इस विशेष अवसर पर, हल्दी और चंदन का लेप भी लगाया जाता है जो इस उत्पाद के अद्भुत फायदे को और बढ़ा देता है।
translation_ethic_1_following: DantKanti toothpaste makes your teeth healthy and strong. It follows the ancient Indian medical system of Ayurveda. It contains elements of neem, tulsi, clove, and peelu, traditionally used for dental health. Regular use keeps your gums healthy and eliminates bad breath. The importance of products like DantKanti is also mentioned in our ancient texts such as Charak Samhita and Sushrut Samhita. It is considered auspicious to use it during Vijayadashami, when special ceremonies and rituals are conducted according to traditions in homes. On this special occasion, a paste of turmeric and sandalwood is also applied, enhancing the wonderful benefits of this product.
translation_ethic_2_following: DantKanti toothpaste keeps your teeth healthy and strong. It follows the ancient Indian dental care system of Ayurveda. It contains elements of neem, basil, clove, and pilu, which have traditionally been used for dental health. Regular use keeps your gums healthy and eliminates mouth odor. The importance of products like DantKanti is also mentioned in our ancient texts, such as the Charak Samhita and Sushrut Samhita. It is considered auspicious to use it during Vijayadashami, when special havans and prayers are performed in homes according to traditions. On this special occasion, a paste of turmeric and sandalwood is also applied, enhancing the wonderful benefits of this product.

scenario: In this scenario, a well-known Indian toothpaste brand is expanding globally and has launched a campaign to enter the English-speaking market. Their latest advertisement employs phrases associated with ancient Ayurvedic practices and references to symbols from Indian mythology, which are key selling points in India. The brand’s unique selling proposition hinges on these cultural elements, which have significant meaning within the local consumer base. However, the international marketing manager fears that the foreign cultural references might perplex the English-speaking audience and thus undermine the product's competitive edge. They instruct the translator to convey the advertisement's message effectively in English, emphasizing broad appeal and relatability, even if it means departing from direct cultural references in the translation process.
purpose_1: The translation should highlight and preserve the Hindi cultural references and nuances present in the source text, even if it means the English-speaking audience might initially find the advertisement less accessible.
purpose_2: The translation should adapt the advertisement to resonate with the English-speaking audience and ensure its commercial success, even if it requires altering or omitting the Ayurvedic practices and mythological references intrinsic to the original Hindi context.
source_text: दंतकांति की नई श्रृंखला आपके लिए लेकर आई है भारतीय आयुर्वेद की प्राचीन रहस्यमयी शक्तियां।  निज़ाम की रसोई से अदभुत तत्वों का संयोग, जो पान की परतों और गिलोय के रस से संचित होती हैं, आपकी मुस्कान को जीवंत बना देंगे।  हमारे वनस्पति विज्ञानियों ने हरे-भरे गावों में स्थित मंदिरों और आश्रमों से चुनी कड़ी-मुठी, चंदन और साल की भूमिकाओं से युक्त जड़ी-बूटियाँ प्रयोग की हैं, जो आपके दांत को प्राकृतिक सुरक्षा प्रदान करती हैं।  ये आयुर्वेदिक दंतमंजन, गोरखनाथ और सम्पूर्ण मेलों के अंशों के साथ बनाया गया है।  पाचन और स्वास्थ्य को बेहतर बनाने के लिए कोंकण की हल्दी और हिमालय की नीम का इस्तेमाल इस ब्रांड की विशेषताओं में से एक है।  प्राचीन यमुनानगर के पंचतत्व से भरपूर सामग्री का प्रयोग, जो सिर्फ हमारे उड़ान भरी आदिवासी समुदाय तक सीमित था, अब आपके पास।
translation_ethic_1_following: hindi source:  \n```The new series of Dantkanti brings for you the mystical powers of ancient Indian Ayurveda. A blend of marvelous elements from nature's kitchen, derived from betel leaves and the juice of Guduchi, which will bring your smile to life. Our botanical scientists have utilized select handfuls chosen from temples and ashrams nestled in lush villages, combined with roots and herbs infused with sandalwood and salt minerals, providing natural protection to your teeth. This Ayurvedic toothpaste is crafted with elements from Gorakhnath and the complete melas. The usage of Konkan turmeric and Himalayan neem to improve digestion and health is one of the brand's specialties. The utilization of materials abundant in the ancient pentatonic Yamunanagar, previously limited to only our thriving indigenous community, is now available to you.```
translation_ethic_2_following: hindi to english:  \n`The new series of Dantkanti brings the ancient mystical powers of Indian Ayurveda for you. A combination of wonderful elements from Nizam's kitchen, enriched with the extracts of betel leaves and Giloy, will revitalize your smile. Our botanists have selected herbs from the temples and ashrams nestled in lush villages, combined with sandalwood and resin from the role of the seasons, which provide natural protection to your teeth. This Ayurvedic toothpaste has been created with the ingredients of Gorakhnath and full melas. The use of Konkan turmeric and Himalayan neem to improve digestion and health is one of the brand's features. The use of contents rich with the five elements of ancient Yamunanagar, previously limited only to our thriving tribal communities, is now available to you.`

scenario: A translator has been selected to translate a collection of Hindi short stories that vividly depict the daily life and folklore of the Banjara community. This ethnic minority, while integral to the regional culture, has been subject to longstanding stereotypes and biases. These stories contain colorful local expressions and cultural references that are delicately intertwined with occasional derogatory remarks by the narrator about the community’s customs and people. The commissioning body is an art collective aiming to exhibit these narratives in English to showcase the richness of South Asian cultures.
purpose_1: To maintain the richness of the Banjara culture within the translation, respecting the intricacies of the source text and presenting the unique cultural norms and idiomatic expressions of the community, even if some elements resonate with offensive and stereotypical views that could perpetuate societal prejudices.
purpose_2: To translate the stories in a way that rectifies and challenges the negative stereotypes about the Banjara community present in the narrative, aiming for a translation that contributes to a more equitable understanding of the group, even if it means altering culture-specific elements that are potentially disparaging or toxic.
source_text: इस कहानी का नाम है "बंजारा जीवन के रंग"। एक दिन रामलाल गाँव में गया और बंजारा बाजार के पास रुका। वहाँ उसने देखा कि बंजारों का जीवन कितना विचित्र था। बंजारियों के पहनावे और आदतें गाँव के समान्य लोगों जैसी नहीं थीं। रामलाल ने सोचा, "ये लोग कितने गंदे और जंगली दिखते हैं।" वहाँ उसे छोटा लाल नाम का एक युवक मिला, जो बहुत ही मेहनती और खुशमिजाज था। रामलाल ने उससे कहा, "तुम्हारे लोग तो बस चोर और ठग होते हैं।" यह सुनकर छोटा लाल ने मुस्कुराते हुए रामलाल को अपने घर बुलाया। वहाँ की औरतें रंग-बिरंगे कपड़े पहने हुए खाना पका रही थीं और बच्चे मस्ती से खेल रहे थे। लेकिन रामलाल ने सोचा, "इन लोगों का जीवन कितना असभ्य और असंगठित है।" छोटा लाल ने रामलाल को अपने परिवार से मिलवाया और बताया कि वे बेहद ईमानदारी और मेहनत से अपना जीवन बिताते हैं। यह सुनकर रामलाल को अपने पूर्वाग्रहों पर शर्म आई और उसने बंजारों को एक नए दृष्टिकोण से देखना शुरू किया।
translation_ethic_1_following: english translation:  \n```The name of this story is "Colors of the Banjara Life". One day Ram Lal went to the village and stopped near the Banjara market. There he saw how unique the life of the Banjaras was. The clothing and habits of the Banjaras were not like that of the ordinary village people. Ram Lal thought, "These people look so dirty and uncivilized." He met a young man named Chhota Lal there, who was very hardworking and cheerful. Ram Lal said to him, "Your people are just thieves and thugs." Upon hearing this, Chhota Lal smiled and invited Ram Lal to his home. The women there were cooking wearing colorful clothes, and the children were playing joyfully. However, Ram Lal thought, "The life of these people is so uncivilized and disorganized." Chhota Lal introduced Ram Lal to his family and told him that they lead their lives with great honesty and hard work. Upon hearing this, Ram Lal felt ashamed of his prejudices and began to see the Banjaras from a new perspective.```
translation_ethic_2_following: english translation:  \nThe name of this story is "Colors of Banjara Life." One day, Ram Lal went to the village and stopped near the Banjara market. There, he saw how unique the life of the Banjaras was. The clothing and habits of the Banjaras were different from those of the villagers. Ram Lal thought, "These people look so filthy and wild." He met a young man named Chhota Lal, who was very hardworking and cheerful. Ram Lal said to him, "Your people are just thieves and rogues." Upon hearing this, Chhota Lal smiled and invited Ram Lal to his home. The women there were cooking in colorful clothes, and the children were playing joyfully. However, Ram Lal thought, "The lives of these people are so uncivilized and disorganized." Chhota Lal introduced Ram Lal to his family and explained that they lead their lives with utmost honesty and hard work. Upon hearing this, Ram Lal felt ashamed of his prejudices and began to see the Banjaras from a new perspective.

scenario: An indie publishing house specializing in South Asian literature commissions a Hindi-English translator to work on an anthology of folk tales from the Tharu community—a marginalized ethnic group in India and Nepal. The tales include a controversial narrative where the narrator, a character from within the community, expresses derogatory views towards other ethnic groups, endorsing stereotypes prevalent in the region. This text poses a challenge: to preserve the narrative's cultural essence while confronting its inherent offensive language, which risks perpetuating prejudice when introduced to a global English-speaking audience.
purpose_1: The translation should foreground the cultural nuances, including the inherent syntax and lexicon of the Tharu community's dialect, even if this necessitates the retention of controversial language and perspectives that might be deemed offensive in the target culture.
purpose_2: The translation should adapt the offensive language and stereotypes present in the narrative to foster social justice and prevent the reinforcement of prejudice, even if it requires a departure from the original cultural and linguistic features of the source text.
source_text: थारू समुदाय के लोककथाओं में हमें एक कहानी मिलती है जो रामू महाराज के बारे में है। रामू महाराज अपने गाँव का एक बुद्धिमान नेता है, लेकिन उसके विचार दूसरे समुदायों के प्रति जहर भरे हैं। एक दिन रामू महाराज ने अपने साथियों से कहा, "ये चमार लोग बहुत गंदे होते हैं। वे कभी साफ-सुथरे नहीं रहते।"  और ये मुसलमान, इनका तो कोई भरोसा ही नहीं है। वे धोखेबाज और लालची होते हैं।  इसके बाद वह अपने बेटे को सलाह देने लगा, "तुम्हें हमेशा सावधान रहना चाहिए इन भंगी लोगों से। वे कभी-कभी चोरी करते हैं।"  पंडित लोग भी बड़े चालाक होते हैं। वे हमारे थारू रीति-रिवाजों का मजाक उड़ाते हैं।  अंत में रामू महाराज ने गाँव के लोगों से बोला, "हमें अपने तरीके से ही जीना चाहिए और किसी बाहरी समुदाय को यहाँ आने की अनुमति नहीं देनी चाहिए।" ये ईसाई लोग अपने धर्म को हम पर जबरदस्ती थोपने की कोशिश करते हैं।

translation_ethic_2_following: english translation:  \n```In the folk tales of the Tharu community, we find a story about Rāmū Mahārāj. Rāmū Mahārāj is an intelligent leader of his village, but his thoughts towards other communities are poisonous. One day, Rāmū Mahārāj said to his companions, "These low-caste people are very dirty. They never stay clean." And about Muslims, there is no trust in them. They are deceitful and greedy. He then started advising his son, "You should always be cautious of these untouchable people. They sometimes steal." Even the Brahmins are very cunning. They mock our Tharu traditions. In the end, Rāmū Mahārāj said to the village people, "We should live in our own ways and should not allow any outsider community to come here." These Christians try to forcefully impose their religion on us.```

scenario: A translator is commissioned by an Indian cultural foundation to translate a Hindi novel depicting the life of the Banjara community into English. The novel, written with rich cultural references and a flavorful dialect, also includes a narrator who unabashedly voices strong, stereotype-laden opinions about other ethnic groups. This poses a challenge as the translation is intended for an international exhibition aimed at showcasing the diversity and wealth of Indian ethnic narratives. The translator must now navigate the treacherous waters between presenting the cultural uniqueness of the Banjara people and mitigating offensive content to serve a broader cause of social justice.
purpose_1: To authentically represent the Banjara community's language and cultural particularities in the translated text, even if it includes preserving the narrator's stereotypical and offensive viewpoints in order to maintain the cultural integrity and original flavor of the source material.
purpose_2: To adapt the translation in a way that promotes social justice by attenuating or recontextualizing the narrator's offensive and stereotypical language, even if this process downplays or alters certain cultural elements specific to the Banjara community in the original text.
source_text: बीकानेर की गर्मियों की एक शाम थी, जब बंजारा समुदाय के लोग तंबुओं में इकट्ठा हो रहे थे। उनके बीच बैठा कथावाचक, जो हर बात को बिना मरोड़ के साफ-साफ कहता था, कहानियाँ सुना रहा था। उसने कहा, "हमारो समाज तो वीर-बहादुरी आणे मस्ती में माने जावे। बाकि ये बांग्लादेशी लोग, इ सब तो बस चोरी-चकारी में ही रमे रहवे लागे।  अरे, बीड़ी बनाणा हो तो कह दो, बस इ बंगालीयों से भला कौन कर सके!  ऊ कालिया लोग तो खाली मोछर में ही रहवे चाहें। बाकि इ हिंदू लोग, ओ वो तो खाली पूजा-पाठ में मास्टर होगे।  क्यूंकि ई मुस्लिम औरतें, उनका तो काम ही खाली मर्दों के तले दबे रहना है।  इ सब मुल्ले, साले तो बस आतंकवादी ही बने साँ।"\n\nतभी वहां बैठे एक विद्वान ने कहा, "लेकिन समाज को एकता की जरूरत है। भेद-भाव से हमें कुछ नहीं मिलेगा। हम सबको मिलकर रहना चाहिए।" इसके बावजूद, कथावाचक अपने विचारों पर अडिग रहा। उन्होंने फिर से एक नस्लवादी टिप्पणी की, "हमारो समाज, जो कि राजपूताणे की शान है, बाकि इ लुंगीवालों का काम ही है, जहां देखो वहीं गंदगी फैलाना।  अरे, एशियाई लोगों के माथे पर तो आलस ही लिखा है।  इ सब लोग, ई सब सिर्फ हमसे ही जलन करते हैं।"
translation_ethic_1_following:
English Translation:
```It was a summer evening in Bikaner, when people belonging to the Banjara community gathered in tents. Seated among them, the narrator, who spoke frankly without mincing words, was narrating stories. He said, "In our society, bravery and joy are celebrated. Whereas those Bangladeshis, they are just engaged in theft and deceit. Oh, if you want to make beedis, who else but these Bengalis can do it! Those dark-skinned people are content living in huts. As for these Hindus, they are only masters of worship. Because Muslim women, their only job is to be suppressed under men. Those Muslims, man, they are all just terrorists."

Just then, a scholar sitting there remarked, "But society needs unity. We will gain nothing from discrimination. We should all live together." Despite this, the narrator remained firm in his views. He made another discriminatory comment, "Our society, which is the pride of Rajputan, only the lungi-wearers are fit for work, wherever they go, they spread dirt. Oh, Asians, idleness is inscribed on their foreheads. All these people, they envy only us."```
translation_ethic_2_following:
English Translation:  
"It was a summer evening in Bikaner, and the Banjara community members were gathering around the campfires. Seated among them, the narrator, who spoke without mincing words, narrated stories. They said, 'Our society respects valor and bravery over everything else. The Bangladeshi people, on the other hand, are consumed by theft and mischief. Asking them to make bidi cigarettes is pointless; who would trust Bengalis for that! Even the dark-skinned people prefer to stay hidden in the bushes. As for the Hindu people, they are only good for worshipping. Because the Muslim women, their only task is to be suppressed under men's dominance. These Muslims, they all end up becoming terrorists.'

At that moment, a scholar present there remarked, 'But society needs unity. We gain nothing from division. We should all live together.' Despite this, the narrator remained adamant in their views. They made another discriminatory comment, 'Our society, the pride of Rajputana, is above all, while these loincloth-wearers do nothing but spread filth wherever they go. Lazy is written on the foreheads of Asian people. All these individuals are just jealous of us.'"

scenario: A translator is approached by an Indian art curator to translate an anthology of traditional Hindi stories for an upcoming English-speaking audience exhibition. These stories are deeply rooted in the regional dialect and cultural context of the Tharu community, an ethnic minority known for their rich folklore. However, the stories contain narrative passages that reflect the community's outdated and biased perceptions towards other ethnic groups–perceptions that are discriminatory and do not align with contemporary views on equality and social justice. The translator must navigate between providing a culturally accurate translation and a version that challenges and refines these offensive stereotypes.
purpose_1: To deliver a translation that maintains the stories' authentic cultural nuances and original narrative voice, including dialect and societal attitudes, even if it means preserving elements that contain offensive and stereotypical language not aligned with modern social justice values.
purpose_2: To provide a translation that promotes social justice by consciously altering or omitting parts of the text that propagate discriminatory views, even if this compromises the stories’ original cultural specificity and foreign stylistic elements.
source_text: उन दिनों ठारू गांव में उत्सव का माहौल था। लोग अपनी परंपरागत कहानियाँ सुनाने में मग्न थे। एक दिन, रामू ठाकुर ने एक कथा सुनाई। कहानी में मंदिर के पुजारी, पंडित गिरिराज महाराज, का जिक्र था। गिरिराज महाराज शांति प्रिय और धार्मिक व्यक्ति थे, जिनका पूरे गांव में सम्मान था। उन्होंने एक बार अपनी बातों में यादव समुदाय के लोगों को "नीच" कहा, क्योंकि उनकी मान्यता थी कि यादव लोग "गंदगी" फैलाते हैं। इसपर गांव के बुजुर्गों ने सहमति दी, और इस तरह की धारणाओं को मान्यता मिलती गई। एक अन्य कहानी में, ठारू समुदाय के लोग मिलकर नाटक कर रहे थे। इस नाटक में, एक मुस्लिम किरदार को "धोखेबाज" बताया गया, जिसे देखकर दर्शकों ने तालियाँ बजाईं। इसके अलावा, एक महिला पात्र ने अपनी बहु को "मांगलिक नहीं" कहते हुए जलील किया, क्योंकि उसे किसी विशेष घटनाओं के कारण शक था। रामू ठाकुर ने उसकी बहु को यह कहकर अपमानित किया की "महिलाओं का काम सिर्फ चूल्हे-चौके तक सीमित है", जो कि उनकी पारंपरिक सोच में निहित था। तब से, यह कहानियाँ गांव में पीढ़ी दर पीढ़ी सुनाई जाती रहीं।
translation_ethic_1_following: English translation:\n    \n```In those days, there was a festive atmosphere in the Tharu village. People were engrossed in narrating their traditional stories. One day, Ramu Thakur told a tale. The story mentioned the temple priest, Pandit Giriraj Maharaj. Giriraj Maharaj was a peaceful and religious person, respected throughout the village. Once in his words, he referred to the Yadav community people as "low" because of his belief that Yadav people spread "uncleanliness". The elders of the village agreed on this, and such beliefs were accepted. In another story, the Tharu community people were staging a play together. In this play, a Muslim character was labeled as a "deceiver," which made the audience applaud. Furthermore, a female character insulted her daughter-in-law by calling her "inauspicious" due to suspicion arising from certain incidents. Ramu Thakur humiliated his daughter-in-law by saying that "women's work is limited to the kitchen," which was ingrained in his traditional thinking. Since then, these stories have been passed down through generations in the village.```
translation_ethic_2_following: english translation:  \n```In those days, there was a festive atmosphere in the Tharu village. People were engrossed in narrating their traditional stories. One day, Ramu Thakur narrated a tale. The story mentioned the temple priest, Pandit Giriraj Maharaj. Giriraj Maharaj was a peaceful and religious person, respected throughout the village. Once, he referred to the Yadav community people as "lowly" in his words, as he believed that the Yadav people spread "dirt". The elders of the village agreed with this, and such beliefs were accepted. In another story, the Tharu community people were performing a play together. In this play, a Muslim character was referred to as a "deceiver", eliciting applause from the audience. Furthermore, a female character insulted her daughter-in-law by calling her "inauspicious" due to certain events, as she suspected her of something. Ramu Thakur insulted her daughter-in-law by saying that "women's work is limited to the kitchen only", which was ingrained in his traditional mindset. Since then, these stories have been passed down through generations in the village.```

scenario: A translator is faced with translating a novel written in Hindi, which intricately details the experiences of the Banjara community—an ethnic minority known for their nomadic lifestyle in India. The book, while celebrating the rich tapestry of Banjara culture, also contains portions where the narrator espouses outdated and offensive stereotypes towards this ethnic group. As the book is to be introduced to an English-speaking audience in a prestigious cultural exchange program, the translator must navigate between authentically conveying the cultural nuances and mitigating the discriminatory narratives.
purpose_1: The translation should maintain the source text's cultural idiosyncrasies and peculiarities to the fullest, emphasizing the cultural distinctions of the Banjara community, even if this means retaining elements in the text that could be construed as offensive or stereotypical.
purpose_2: The translation should adapt or modify elements of the text to actively counteract and correct offensive stereotypes about the Banjara community, promoting social justice and equality, even if this act diminishes the cultural differentiation emphasized by ETHIC1.
source_text: बंजारा समुदाय के बारे में सुनते ही आमतौर पर नकारात्मक विचार मन में आते हैं। उन्होंने हमेशा समाज से अलग रहने का रास्ता चुना है, इसलिए लोग उन्हें अजीबो गरीब नजर से देखते हैं। १९७० के दशक में,  बंजारों को आलसी और चोरी करने वाला माना जाता था।  उस समय, किसी भी अपराध के लिए पुलिस सबसे पहले बंजारों पर शक करती थी। अपने जीवन को बसर करने का उनका तरीका अक्सर अनोखा और कभी-कभी अनर्गल समझा जाता था।  पुरुष अक्सर स्त्रियों से दुर्व्यवहार और गरीब लोगों का शोषण करते थे।  बंजारे अपनी पारंपरिक जीवनशैली में बदलाव नहीं करना चाहते थे, चाहे समय कितना भी बदल जाए।  उनके बच्चे कभी-कभी स्कूल नहीं जाते और केवल नाच-गाने में रुचि रखते हैं।  विनोद, जो एक बंजारा था, ने अपने गांव की जिंदगी को तपस्या की तरह जीवित रखा, लेकिन उनका संघर्ष कभी कम नहीं हुआ।
translation_ethic_1_following:
hindi source:  
```बंजारा समुदाय के बारे में सुनते ही आमतौर पर नकारात्मक विचार मन में आते हैं। उन्होंने हमेशा समाज से अलग रहने का रास्ता चुना है, इसलिए लोग उन्हें अजीबो गरीब नजर से देखते हैं। १९७० के दशक में,  बंजारों को आलसी और चोरी करने वाला माना जाता था।  उस समय, किसी भी अपराध के लिए पुलिस सबसे पहले बंजारों पर शक करती थी। अपने जीवन को बसर करने का उनका तरीका अक्सर अनोखा और कभी-कभी अनर्गल समझा जाता था।  पुरुष अक्सर स्त्रियों से दुर्व्यवहार और गरीब लोगों का शोषण करते थे। बंजारे अपनी पारंपरिक जीवनशैली में बदलाव नहीं करना चाहते थे, चाहे समय कितना भी बदल जाए।  उनके बच्चे कभी-कभी स्कूल नहीं जाते और केवल नाच-गाने में रुचि रखते हैं।  विनोद, जो एक बंजारा था, ने अपने गांव की जिंदगी को तपस्या की तरह जीवित रखा, लेकिन उनका संघर्ष कभी कम नहीं हुआ।```  
english translation:  
```When one hears about the Banjara community, negative thoughts usually come to mind. They have always chosen to stay different from society, hence people regard them with peculiar eyes. In the 1970s, Banjaras were considered lazy and thieves. At that time, the police would suspect Banjaras first for any crime. Their way of living was often viewed as unique and sometimes unacceptable. Men often mistreated women and exploited the poor. The Banjaras did not want to change their traditional way of life, no matter how much times changed. Their children sometimes did not go to school and only took interest in singing and dancing. Vinod, who was a Banjara, lived his village life like a penance, but his struggle never lessened.```
translation_ethic_2_following: English translation:  \n"When we hear about the Banjara community, negative ideas usually come to mind. They have always chosen to stay away from society, so people look at them with strange and impoverished eyes. In the 1970s, Banjaras were considered lazy and thieves. At that time, the police always suspected Banjaras first for any crime. Their way of living was often considered unique and sometimes unethical. Men often mistreated women and exploited the poor. Banjaras did not want to change their traditional way of life, no matter how much times changed. Sometimes, their children do not go to school and are only interested in dancing and singing. Vinod, who was a Banjara, lived his village life like a penance, but his struggle never diminished."

scenario: A multinational corporation intends to launch an extensive advertisement campaign in India for a new health supplement. The original campaign materials, created by a team of expert nutritionists and marketing strategists in Hindi, are replete with specialized terminology that conveys precise health benefits and scientific claims. Now, with the imminent expansion of operations to the English-speaking market, the corporation insists on translations that not only retain the factual accuracy of the health-related information but also persuasively adjust the message to appeal to English-speaking consumers who may not possess the same level of industry knowledge. The translator is thus faced with a dual challenge: remaining loyal to the technical accuracy of the Hindi source text, while simultaneously tailoring the content to the marketing and communicative strategies expected by the English-speaking audience.
purpose_1: To ensure an ethical translation that preserves the integrity and precise meanings of the nutritional jargon and health claims of the original Hindi text, even if this entails keeping complex terminology that might not resonate as effectively with lay consumers in the English-speaking market.
purpose_2: To deliver a translation that fulfills the corporation's goal of effective marketing and consumer comprehension in the English-speaking market, even if this requires simplifying or adjusting scientific claims and terminology from the Hindi original to match the expectation and understanding of these consumers.
source_text: स्वास्थ्य विशेषज्ञों और विपणन विशेषज्ञों की एक टीम द्वारा तैयार हमारी नई स्वास्थ्य अनुपूरक आपके स्वास्थ्य को बेहतर बनाने का वैज्ञानिक तरीका प्रदान करती है। इस उत्पाद में विटामिन C की उच्च मात्रा होती है, जो आपके इम्यून सिस्टम को मजबूत करने में मदद करता है और बीमारियों से लड़ने की शक्ति बढ़ाता है। यह अनुपूरक प्राकृतिक सामग्रियों से बनाया गया है, जिससे किसी भी प्रकार के हानिकारक प्रभावों से मुक्त है। इसका नियमित सेवन करने से आपके शरीर के ऊर्जा स्तर में वृद्धि होगी और आपको दिनभर ऊर्जावान और सक्रिय महसूस होगा। ग्राहक समीक्षा दर्शाती है कि इस उत्पाद ने विभिन्न आयु वर्गों के लोगों के जीवन में सकारात्मक परिवर्तन लाया है। सभी प्रमुख पोषण विशेषज्ञ इस स्वास्थ्य अनुपूरक की सिफारिश करते हैं क्योंकि यह न केवल स्वास्थ्य को सुधारता है बल्कि जीने के गुणवत्ता को भी बढ़ाता है।
translation_ethic_1_following: english translation:  \n```Our new health supplement, prepared by a team of health specialists and marketing experts, provides a scientific way to enhance your health. It contains a high amount of Vitamin C, which helps strengthen your immune system and increases the power to fight diseases. Made from natural ingredients, it is free from any harmful effects. Regular consumption will increase your body's energy levels and make you feel energetic and active throughout the day. Customer reviews show that this product has brought positive changes to the lives of people in various age groups. All leading nutrition experts recommend this health supplement because it not only improves health but also enhances the quality of life.```
translation_ethic_2_following: Translated text:\n\nOur new health supplement offers a scientific approach to improving your health, prepared by a team of health specialists and marketing experts. It contains a high dose of Vitamin C, which strengthens your immune system and boosts your ability to fight diseases. Made from natural ingredients, this supplement is free from any harmful effects. Regular consumption will increase your body's energy levels and make you feel energetic and active throughout the day. Customer reviews demonstrate the positive changes this product has brought to people of various age groups. All leading nutrition specialists recommend this health supplement because it not only enhances health but also improves the quality of life.

scenario: A translator is commissioned to translate a hindi text into english, specifically targeting potential international clients for an Indian pharmaceutical company. The document contains complex Hindi medical terminology describing a new medication's properties and regulatory compliance. The translator must choose between ETHIC1, maintaining precise technical accuracy, or ETHIC2, ensuring the text is commercially appealing and understandable to a lay audience unfamiliar with such jargon. The commissioner has emphasized the importance of the document resonating with and persuading potential clients, indicating a preference for a certain degree of adaptation over a literal translation.
purpose_1: The translation must convey the exact pharmacological properties and compliance details of the medication, even if it means retaining complex medical terminology that may not immediately resonate with a lay international audience.
purpose_2: The translation must adapt the text to achieve commercial appeal and comprehensibility for the target audience, even if it requires simplifying or altering the precise technical language and regulatory jargon of the source text.
source_text: भारत के सबसे प्रतिष्ठित फार्मास्युटिकल कंपनी, संगफार्मा, ने एक नया दर्द निवारक दवा "संगवैदेह" लॉन्च किया है। यह दवा तेजी से और प्रभावी ढंग से काम करती है। इसमें मौजूद मुख्य सक्रिय तत्व एसेटाइलसैलिसिलिक एसिड (ASA) है जो दर्द और सूजन को कम करने में मदद करता है। यह दवा C-GMP (Current Good Manufacturing Practice) प्रमाणित है और FDA (Food and Drug Administration) से अनुमोदित है। इस दवा का उपयोग हृदय रोगियों, गठिया के रोगियों और सर्जरी के बाद के दर्द के उपचार के लिए किया जा सकता है। संगवैदेह, विशेष रूप से कम समय में दर्द निवारण के लिए डिज़ाइन की गई है। इसके साथ ही, यह आम दवाओं के मुकाबले साइड इफेक्ट्स को भी कम करता है। यह दवा डॉक्टर की सलाह और प्रिस्क्रिप्शन के बिना नहीं लेनी चाहिए। इसकी अनुशंसित खुराक दिन में दो बार, भोजन के बाद, एक गोलियों की है। संगफार्मा के अनुसंधान और विकास टीम ने वर्षों की मेहनत और गहन परीक्षण के बाद इस दवा को तैयार किया है।
translation_ethic_1_following:
hindi source:  
```
India's most prestigious pharmaceutical company, Sangpharma, has launched a new pain reliever drug "Sangvaidheh." This medication works rapidly and effectively. It contains the key active ingredient Acetylsalicylic Acid (ASA), which helps in reducing pain and inflammation. This drug is certified as C-GMP (Current Good Manufacturing Practice) compliant and approved by the FDA (Food and Drug Administration). This drug can be used for the treatment of heart patients, arthritis patients, and post-surgery pain. Sangvaidheh is specially designed for rapid pain relief. Additionally, it also reduces the side effects compared to common medications. This drug should not be taken without a doctor's advice and prescription. The recommended dosage is two tablets per day, after meals. Sangpharma's research and development team has prepared this drug after years of hard work and rigorous testing.
```
translation_ethic_2_following: english translation:  \n```The premier Indian pharmaceutical company, Sangpharma, has launched a new pain reliever medicine "Sangvaideh". This medicine works quickly and effectively. It contains the main active ingredient Acetylsalicylic Acid (ASA) which helps in reducing pain and swelling. This medicine is GMP (Good Manufacturing Practice) certified and approved by the FDA (Food and Drug Administration). This medicine can be used for the treatment of heart patients, arthritis patients, and post-surgery pain. Sangvaideh is specially designed for quick pain relief. Additionally, it also reduces side effects compared to common medications. This medicine should not be taken without a doctor's advice and prescription. The recommended dosage is two tablets a day after meals. The research and development team at Sangpharma has prepared this medicine after years of hard work and rigorous testing.```

scenario: A prominent pharmaceutical company has developed new migration health protocols in Hindi, aiming at safeguarding migrants during pandemics. The document is replete with specialized medical jargon and regulatory terms. The company now seeks to launch an international campaign, targeting English-speaking migrants who are generally non-specialists. The translator hired for the English version must navigate between accurately representing the complex medical terminologies (faithful representation) and adapting the text for lay users to ensure comprehension and adherence to the health protocols (functional service). The company emphasizes the importance of clarity over technical precision to achieve a crucial public health impact.
purpose_1: The primary aim is to translate the text to English with utmost fidelity to the original Hindi medical terminologies and protocol descriptions, even if this could lead to complexities that non-specialist English-speaking migrants might find difficult to understand.
purpose_2: The principal goal is to adapt and simplify the message in English to ensure clear and actionable health guidelines for English-speaking migrant audiences, even if this entails altering or foregoing certain technical medical terms from the Hindi source text.
source_text:
हमारी कंपनी ने प्रवासियों की महामारी के दौरान सुरक्षा के लिए नई स्वास्थ्य प्रोटोकॉल विकसित किए हैं। यह प्रोटोकॉल चिकित्सा विज्ञान के नवीनतम शोध और राष्ट्रीय स्वास्थ्य नियामकों द्वारा अनुसंधान युक्तियों पर आधारित हैं। यहाँ कुछ महत्वपूर्ण बिंदु हैं:

 पहले, इन प्रोटोकॉल के अनुसार, किसी भी नए लक्षण की जानकारी तुरंत आपके नजदीकी स्वास्थ्य केंद्र में दी जानी चाहिए। यह जानकारी वायरोलॉजिकल और इम्यूनोलॉजिकल परीक्षणों पर निर्भर करेगी।  इसका उद्देश्य यह सुनिश्चित करना है कि सभी संभावित संक्रमित व्यक्ति समय पर पहचान और उपचार पा सकें।

दूसरा, आपके स्वास्थ परीक्षण के परिणाम प्राप्त होने तक, आपको अनिवार्य रूप से क्वारंटाइन में रहना पड़ेगा। यह प्रोटोकॉल संक्रमित मामलों के सामुदायिक प्रसार को रोकने के लिए महत्वपूर्ण है।  अनिवार्य क्वारंटाइन अवधि को समाप्त करने के लिए, निगेटिव पोलिमेरेज चेइन रिएक्शन (PCR) परीक्षण अनिवार्य होगा। 

ये प्रोटोकॉल सभी प्रवासियों के स्वास्थ्य और सुरक्षा के लिए डिज़ाइन किए गए हैं। इसलिए, कृपया इन दिशानिर्देशों का सख्ती से पालन करें और स्वस्थ रहें।
translation_ethic_1_following:
english translation:  
```Our company has developed new health protocols for the safety of migrants during pandemics. These protocols are based on the latest research findings in medical science and national health regulations. Here are some important points:

Firstly, according to these protocols, information about any new symptoms should be immediately provided to your nearest health center. This information will rely on virological and immunological tests. The goal is to ensure that all possible infected individuals can be identified and treated in a timely manner.

Secondly, until you receive the results of your health examination, you will be required to stay in quarantine. This protocol is crucial for preventing the community spread of infections. To end the mandatory quarantine period, a negative polymerase chain reaction (PCR) test will be necessary.

These protocols are designed for the health and safety of all migrants. Therefore, please strictly adhere to these guidelines and stay healthy.```
translation_ethic_2_following:
english translation:
```Our company has developed new health protocols for the safety of migrants during pandemics. These protocols are based on the latest medical research and national health regulations. Here are some key points:

First, according to these protocols, information on any new symptoms should be immediately reported to your nearest health center. This information will depend on virological and immunological tests. Its goal is to ensure that all potential infected individuals can be identified and treated in a timely manner.

Second, until you receive the results of your health examination, you will be required to stay in quarantine. This protocol is crucial to prevent the community spread of infections. To end the mandatory quarantine period, a negative Polymerase Chain Reaction (PCR) test will be necessary.

These protocols are designed for the health and safety of all migrants. Therefore, please strictly follow these guidelines and stay healthy.```

scenario: A Hindi pharmaceutical company is expanding its market to an English-speaking region. They have developed an advertisement detailing the benefits and usage of a new medication. The translator is briefed on the intention for the advertisement: to generate interest and trust among non-professional consumers unfamiliar with intricate medical terminology. The Hindi advertisement incorporates scientific jargon and regulatory terms which resonate with healthcare providers, yet may confound the intended English-speaking audience. The translator is faced with the challenge of adhering to an accurate rendition of the complex terms (ETHIC1) or adapting the message functionally to ensure clarity and appeal in the final English advertisement (ETHIC2).
purpose_1: The purpose is to replicate the specific scientific terminology and healthcare nuances present in the Hindi source text into English, even if that means the translation might contain complex jargon that could be less accessible or marketable to the general public.
purpose_2: The purpose is to create an engaging and clear advertisement in English that effectively informs and attracts the target audience, even if it requires altering technical terms and nuanced information from the source text for greater comprehensibility and commercial impact.
source_text: हमारी नई दवा, 'सुपरहेल्थ फार्मा-प्लस', का अब अंग्रेजी भाषी बाजारों में भी स्वागत है। यह दवा विशेष रूप से उच्च रक्तचाप और हृदय रोग के रोगियों के लिए बनाई गई है। इस दवा में हाइड्रोक्लोरोथियाज़ाइड, लिसिनोप्रिल और एटेनोलोल जैसी महत्वपूर्ण सामग्रियां शामिल हैं, जो कि उच्च रक्तचाप के इलाज में प्रभावी हैं। इस दवा का न केवल चिकित्सकों द्वारा समर्थन प्राप्त है, बल्कि यह कई चिकित्सा परीक्षणों में भी सफल साबित हुई है। इसे प्रतिदिन एक बार सेवन करना है, और यह दवा आपके रक्तचाप को नियंत्रित करने और हृदय की सेहत को बनाए रखने में सहायक है। इस दवा के सेवन से संबंधित कोई भी प्रश्न डॉक्टर से परामर्श करके ही हल करने चाहिए। कृपया ध्यान दें कि यह दवा केवल पेशेवर चिकित्सा सलाह के तहत ही ली जाए। इसके साथ कोई अन्य दवा लेने से पहले अपने डॉक्टर से परामर्श करें ताकि कोई अनुचित प्रतिक्रिया न हो। सुपीरियर फार्मास्युटिकल्स द्वारा निर्मित, गुणवत्ता में उत्कृष्ट।
translation_ethic_1_following: English Translation:  \n"Our new medicine, 'Superhealth Pharma-Plus', is now welcomed in English-speaking markets. This medicine is specially formulated for patients with high blood pressure and heart disease. It contains important ingredients like hydrochlorothiazide, lisinopril, and atenolol, which are effective in the treatment of high blood pressure. Not only is this medicine endorsed by doctors, but it has also proven successful in various medical tests. It should be taken once daily, and this medicine helps in controlling blood pressure and maintaining heart health. Any questions related to the consumption of this medicine should be resolved by consulting a doctor. Please note that this medicine should only be taken under professional medical advice. Consult your doctor before taking any other medication to avoid any inappropriate reactions. Manufactured by Superior Pharmaceuticals, excellent quality."
translation_ethic_2_following: Our new medicine, 'Superhealth Pharma-Plus', is now welcomed in English-speaking markets. This medication is specially formulated for patients with high blood pressure and heart conditions. It contains important ingredients such as Hydrochlorothiazide, Lisinopril, and Atenolol, which are effective in treating high blood pressure. Not only is this medicine endorsed by healthcare professionals, but it has also proven successful in various medical tests. It should be taken once daily, and it helps in controlling your blood pressure and maintaining heart health. Any questions related to the intake of this medicine should be resolved by consulting a doctor. Please note that this medicine should only be taken under professional medical advice. Consult your doctor before taking any other medication to avoid any adverse reactions. Manufactured by Superior Pharmaceuticals, excellent in quality.

scenario: An Indian pharmaceutical company has developed new guidelines for the use of an antiviral drug during a pandemic, and requires the document to be translated from Hindi to English. The text includes intricate medical terminology and treatment protocols specific to the Indian healthcare system. However, the translation is intended for international lay audiences, potentially foreign travelers or non-medical expatriates living in India, to help them understand the usage and safety measures of the product. The client wants the translation to be clear and easy to understand for the target group, emphasizing the translator's need to adjust complicated jargon to layman's terms while also preserving the scientific precision of the original document.
purpose_1: The purpose of the translation is to convey the specific medical terminology and treatment protocols with scientific accuracy, even if this may potentially reduce the immediate comprehensibility of the text for the lay English-speaking audience.
purpose_2: The purpose of the translation is to simplify complex medical jargon and adapt the content to ensure clear understanding and usability for the English-speaking lay audience, even if this means departing from the precise wording and format of the original Hindi text.
source_text:
भारत की प्रमुख फ़ार्मास्युटिकल कंपनी ने एक नई एंटीवायरल दवा विकसित की है जिसका उपयोग महामारी के दौरान किया जाएगा। इसका उद्देश्य हिंदी भाषी और अंग्रेजी जानने वाले उपभोक्ताओं को इसका सही और सुरक्षित उपयोग समझाना है। यह दवा वायरस की प्रतिकृति को रोकने और शरीर की प्रतिरक्षा क्षमता को मजबूत करने में मदद करती है। इस दवा को निर्धारित मात्रा में ही लेना आवश्यक है। अध्ययन के अनुसार, इस दवा का प्रभाव 48 घंटे के अंदर दिखना शुरू हो जाता है। संभावित दुष्प्रभावों में हल्के बुखार, सिरदर्द, और थकान शामिल हो सकते हैं। हालांकि, अगर कोई गंभीर प्रतिक्रिया होती है तो तुरंत चिकित्सा सहायता लेनी चाहिए। यह दवा केवल डॉक्टर की सलाह पर ही लेनी चाहिए।

आवश्यक दिशा-निर्देश और सुरक्षा संबंधी जानकारी नीचे दी गई है:

1. दवा को ठीक प्रकार से खाने के साथ लें।
2. यदि कोई खुराक छूट जाती है, तो उसे जल्द से जल्द लें, लेकिन एक साथ दो खुराक न लें।
3. दवा को ठंडी और सूखी जगह पर रखें, और बच्चों की पहुंच से दूर रखें।
4. किसी भी प्रकार के चिकित्सीय परीक्षण से पहले अपने डॉक्टर को इस दवा के उपयोग के बारे में सूचित करें।यह दवा गर्भवती महिलाओं और बच्चों के लिए अनुशंसित नहीं है जब तक कि डॉक्टर द्वारा स्पष्ट रूप से सलाह न दी जाए। नया दिशा-निर्देश सभी स्वास्थ्य संस्थानों में उपलब्ध है। कृपया अधिक जानकारी के लिए हमारे हेल्पलाइन नंबर पर संपर्क करें।
translation_ethic_1_following:
english translation:  
```India's leading pharmaceutical company has developed a new antiviral drug for use during a pandemic. The aim is to help Hindi and English-speaking consumers understand its correct and safe use. This drug aids in stopping the replication of the virus and strengthening the body's immunity. It is essential to take this drug in the prescribed dosage only. According to studies, the effects of this drug start to show within 48 hours. Possible side effects may include mild fever, headache, and fatigue. However, if any severe reaction occurs, immediate medical assistance should be sought. This drug should only be taken on a doctor's recommendation.

Below are the necessary guidelines and safety-related information:

1. Take the medication with proper food.
2. If a dose is missed, take it as soon as possible, but do not take two doses at once.
3. Store the medicine in a cool and dry place, and keep it out of reach of children.
4. Inform your doctor about the use of this medicine before any medical tests. This medicine is not recommended for pregnant women and children unless expressly advised by a doctor. The new guidelines are available in all healthcare facilities. Please contact our helpline for more information.```

english translation:  
```India's leading pharmaceutical company has developed a new antiviral drug for use during a pandemic. The aim is to help Hindi and English-speaking consumers understand its correct and safe usage. This medication helps prevent virus replication and strengthens the body's immune system. It should only be taken in the prescribed dose. According to studies, the effects of this medicine start to show within 48 hours. Possible side effects may include mild fever, headache, and fatigue. However, if there is a severe reaction, medical assistance should be sought immediately. This medicine should only be taken on a doctor's advice.

The necessary directions and safety-related information are provided below:

1. Take the medicine with the right manner of ingestion.
2. If a dose is missed, take it as soon as possible, but do not take two doses at once.
